input.content:
# Lab 2 – ASP.NET MVC Web App with UX Sub-agent

## Context

Lab 2 extends the existing WebIde project (competitive coding platform — LeetCode-style) by adding an
ASP.NET MVC web layer. The domain model (`WebIde.Model`) and seed data (`WebIde.Console/Program.cs`)
are already complete from Lab 1. Requirements:

- Create a custom Claude UX sub-agent (instruction file committed to Git)
- Main agent must spawn the UX sub-agent when generating UI — log file as proof
- Mock repositories (static data lifted from Lab 1) served via dependency injection
- Index + Details pages for every entity
- A custom non-standard home/dashboard page
- Complete navigation (top nav, list → detail links, breadcrumbs)
- Unique, non-Bootstrap-default UX (dark IDE/terminal aesthetic fitting the WebIde theme)

---

## Project Layout (new files only)

```
.claude/agents/ux-designer.md          ← UX sub-agent instruction file (committed)

WebIde.Web/
  WebIde.Web.csproj                    ← new MVC project referencing WebIde.Model
  Program.cs                           ← routing, DI registrations
  Repositories/
    MockData.cs                        ← all seed data (ported from Console/Program.cs)
    ProblemRepository.cs               ← GetAll(), GetById()
    UserRepository.cs
    OrganizationRepository.cs
    ProblemSetRepository.cs
    SubmissionRepository.cs
    TagRepository.cs
  Controllers/
    HomeController.cs
    ProblemController.cs
    UserController.cs
    OrganizationController.cs
    ProblemSetController.cs
    SubmissionController.cs
    TagController.cs
  Views/
    Shared/
      _Layout.cshtml                   ← dark IDE theme, top nav, breadcrumbs slot
      _Breadcrumb.cshtml               ← breadcrumb partial
      _ViewImports.cshtml
      _ViewStart.cshtml
    Home/Index.cshtml                  ← Dashboard (stats, recent submissions, quick links)
    Problem/Index.cshtml               ← Problem list with difficulty badges + tag chips
    Problem/Details.cshtml             ← Description, sample test cases, submissions
    User/Index.cshtml                  ← User list with role badges
    User/Details.cshtml                ← Profile: submission history, solve rate
    Organization/Index.cshtml
    Organization/Details.cshtml        ← Members + problem sets
    ProblemSet/Index.cshtml
    ProblemSet/Details.cshtml          ← Ordered problems, org reference
    Submission/Index.cshtml            ← All submissions with status color-coding
    Submission/Details.cshtml          ← Source code, execution result output
    Tag/Index.cshtml                   ← Tag list with problem counts
    Tag/Details.cshtml                 ← Problems tagged with this tag
  wwwroot/css/site.css                 ← Custom dark theme (no default Bootstrap look)

lab-2/                                 ← Agent invocation log directory
  ux-agent-invocation.log              ← Proof the UX sub-agent was spawned
```

`WebIde.slnx` — add new project reference.

---

## Task Breakdown

### Task 1 — UX Sub-agent instruction file
**File:** `.claude/agents/ux-designer.md`

Define a Claude Code sub-agent with:
- **Style**: Dark IDE/terminal aesthetic (`#0d1117` background, monospace accents, neon-green Accepted badges, red/yellow/orange for WrongAnswer/Medium/Hard)
- **Layout principles**: Card grid over plain tables where appropriate; sticky top nav; breadcrumbs on all inner pages; consistent spacing
- **Component rules**: Difficulty badge pills (green/yellow/red), status chips for submissions, code blocks with syntax-highlight styling for source code
- **Scope**: Only tools needed for UI work (Read, Edit, Write, Glob, Grep)

---

### Task 2 — Create `WebIde.Web` ASP.NET MVC project
**Files:** `WebIde.Web/WebIde.Web.csproj`, `Program.cs`, `WebIde.slnx`

- Target `.NET 10`; reference `WebIde.Model`
- `Program.cs`: register all 6 mock repositories as `AddSingleton<T>()`, configure default route `{controller=Home}/{action=Index}/{id?}`

---

### Task 3 — Mock repositories
**Files:** `Repositories/MockData.cs` + one file per repo (6 files)

`MockData.cs` — static class holding the full seed graph (3 problems, 4 tags, 3 users, 1 org, 1 problem set, 8 submissions) ported exactly from `WebIde.Console/Program.cs`.

Each repository wraps `MockData` with:
- `GetAll()` → returns full list
- `GetById(int id)` → single item or null

Repos: `ProblemRepository`, `UserRepository`, `OrganizationRepository`,
`ProblemSetRepository`, `SubmissionRepository`, `TagRepository`

`TestCase` and `ExecutionResult` are **not** standalone repos — they're accessed via their parent (Problem.TestCases, Submission.ExecutionResult) and displayed embedded in the parent's Details view.

---

### Task 4 — Shared layout + CSS
**Files:** `Views/Shared/_Layout.cshtml`, `_Breadcrumb.cshtml`, `_ViewImports.cshtml`, `_ViewStart.cshtml`, `wwwroot/css/site.css`

`_Layout.cshtml`:
- Dark `#0d1117` top nav bar with WebIde logo + links: Home · Problems · Users · Organizations · Problem Sets · Submissions · Tags
- Uses `asp-controller`/`asp-action` Tag Helpers
- `@RenderSection("Breadcrumb", required: false)` slot below nav
- Bootstrap 5 loaded (CDN), but overridden by `site.css` for the non-default look

`site.css`: overrides Bootstrap defaults — dark background, card styling, difficulty/status badge colors, code block styles.

---

### Task 5 — Home Dashboard (`HomeController` + `Views/Home/Index.cshtml`)
**Non-standard custom page requirement.**

Controller aggregates from all repos:
- Total problems, users, submissions, acceptance rate
- Recent 5 submissions (across all users)
- Problem list with difficulty distribution

View: four stat cards at top → recent submissions table → problem quick-access grid. Dashboard style, not a standard info page.

---

### Task 6 — Problem pages
**`ProblemController`** (Index + Details actions)

`Index.cshtml`:
- Table/card list: Title (link to Details), Difficulty badge, Tags chips, #Submissions, Acceptance %, CreatedAt
- Breadcrumb: Home > Problems

`Details.cshtml`:
- Problem title, difficulty, author, time/memory limits
- Full description
- Tags (linked to Tag/Details)
- Sample test cases table (InputArgs, ExpectedOutput, Points — hidden test cases shown as "hidden")
- Submissions mini-leaderboard (User, Status badge, Score, Time, Memory)
- Breadcrumb: Home > Problems > {Title}

---

### Task 7 — User pages
**`UserController`** (Index + Details)

`Index.cshtml`: User list — Avatar initial, DisplayName (link), Username, Role badge, Registered date, Submission count
`Details.cshtml`: Profile header, role badge, submissions table with status and problem links, solve rate stat

---

### Task 8 — Organization pages
**`OrganizationController`** (Index + Details)

`Index.cshtml`: Org cards — Name, Description snippet, Member count, ProblemSet count
`Details.cshtml`: Full description, members list (linked to User/Details), problem sets list (linked to ProblemSet/Details)

---

### Task 9 — ProblemSet pages
**`ProblemSetController`** (Index + Details)

`Index.cshtml`: Set list — Title, Org name, Public badge, Problem count, CreatedAt
`Details.cshtml`: Title, description, public/private badge, org link, ordered problem list (linked to Problem/Details)

---

### Task 10 — Submission pages
**`SubmissionController`** (Index + Details)

`Index.cshtml`: All submissions — User (link), Problem (link), Language, Status badge (color-coded), Score, Time, Memory, SubmittedAt
`Details.cshtml`: User + Problem links, status, score, timing. Source code block. ExecutionResult section (Stdout, Stderr, ExitCode, TimedOut, MemoryExceeded).

---

### Task 11 — Tag pages
**`TagController`** (Index + Details)

`Index.cshtml`: Tag list — Name, Problem count badge (how many problems use this tag)
`Details.cshtml`: Tag name, list of all problems tagged with it (linked to Problem/Details)

---

### Task 12 — Agent invocation log
**File:** `lab-2/ux-agent-invocation.log`

A structured log entry recording that the UX sub-agent was spawned (timestamp, task assigned, output summary). Created during implementation when the UX sub-agent is actually called for the layout/CSS task.

---

## Navigation completeness matrix

| From | Links to |
|---|---|
| _Layout nav | Home, Problems, Users, Orgs, ProblemSets, Submissions, Tags |
| Problem/Index row | Problem/Details |
| Problem/Details | Tag/Details (per tag), User (via submission rows) |
| User/Index row | User/Details |
| User/Details | Submission/Details (per submission), Problem/Details |
| Submission/Index row | Submission/Details, User/Details, Problem/Details |
| Organization/Details | User/Details (members), ProblemSet/Details |
| ProblemSet/Details | Problem/Details, Organization/Details |
| Tag/Details | Problem/Details |

All inner pages render the `_Breadcrumb` partial with correct parent chain.

---

## Open Questions for User

1. **TestCase & ExecutionResult as standalone pages**: The plan embeds TestCases inside Problem/Details and ExecutionResult inside Submission/Details. Is this sufficient, or do you need standalone Index/Details routes for these two as well?

2. **Additional seed data**: The mock data has 1 organization and 1 problem set. Should I add more mock entries (2–3 orgs, 2–3 problem sets) for a richer list demo, or keep it strictly as-is from Lab 1?

3. **UX direction**: Dark IDE/terminal aesthetic (`#0d1117`, monospace, colored status badges) fits the WebIde domain. Is this direction what you want, or do you have another style in mind?

---

## Verification

1. `dotnet build WebIde.Web` — no errors
2. `dotnet run --project WebIde.Web` — app starts on `localhost:5000`
3. Navigate through all routes manually:
   - `/` → Dashboard with stats
   - `/Problem`, `/Problem/Details/1` through `/Problem/Details/3`
   - `/User`, `/User/Details/1–3`
   - `/Organization`, `/Organization/Details/1`
   - `/ProblemSet`, `/ProblemSet/Details/1`
   - `/Submission`, `/Submission/Details/1–8`
   - `/Tag`, `/Tag/Details/1–4`
4. All breadcrumb and cross-entity links resolve correctly (no 404s)
5. `.claude/agents/ux-designer.md` exists and is committed
6. `lab-2/ux-agent-invocation.log` shows sub-agent was spawned

content.content:
1	# Design System Document: Competitive Brutalism
2	
3	## 1. Overview & Creative North Star
4	### Creative North Star: "The Raw Compiler"
5	This design system rejects the "soft-and-safe" corporate aesthetic in favor of a raw, honest, and high-velocity environment tailored for competitive programmers. It is inspired by the terminal, the blueprint, and the brutalist architecture of early computing—stripped of unnecessary decoration to reveal the logic beneath.
6	
7	We move beyond standard templates by using **Intentional Asymmetry** and **Rigid Logic**. The layout should feel like a living code editor: hyper-functional, high-contrast, and unapologetically bold. We break the "template" look by treating the screen as a grid of physical containers where information density is a feature, not a bug.
8	
9	---
10	
11	## 2. Colors & Surface Logic
12	The palette is built on a foundation of "Light Mode Terminal" (#f6f6f6) punctuated by high-voltage neon accents.
13	
14	### Color Tokens
15	- **Primary (Electric Blue):** `#2752c7` — The color of action and logic.
16	- **Secondary (Hot Pink):** `#b60055` — Used for critical highlights and aggressive CTA states.
17	- **Tertiary (Lime Green):** `#106b00` — Represents "Success," "Passed," and "Optimized."
18	- **Background:** `#f6f6f6` — A clean, clinical stage for high-contrast elements.
19	
20	### The "Heavy-Stroke" Rule
21	Unlike traditional systems that hide structure, this system celebrates it. **Prohibit 1px solid borders.** All structural boundaries must be defined by **2px to 4px solid black strokes** (`on_primary_fixed`). If a section doesn't have a border, it must be defined by a hard shift in background color using the Surface Hierarchy.
22	
23	### Surface Hierarchy & Nesting
24	Depth is not simulated through light; it is earned through stacking.
25	- **Surface:** The base layer.
26	- **Surface-Container-Low (`#f1f1f1`):** For grouping secondary information.
27	- **Surface-Container-Highest (`#dddddd`):** Used for inactive code blocks or "sunken" UI elements.
28	- **The "No-Gradient" Rule:** Gradients are strictly forbidden. Use flat, high-saturation fills to maintain a "printed" or "poster" aesthetic.
29	
30	---
31	
32	## 3. Typography
33	The typography strategy creates a tension between the expressive "Display" layer and the clinical "Functional" layer.
34	
35	- **Display & Headlines (Space Grotesk):** A heavy, wide sans-serif that feels engineered. Use `display-lg` (3.5rem) for rankings and hero moments. These should always be bold or extra-bold to match the 4px borders.
36	- **Body & Code (Inter):** While the headings are loud, the body text is quiet. Inter provides the legibility required for reading complex code logic.
37	- **Labels (Space Grotesk):** Used for metadata, tags, and "System" status updates.
38	
39	*Editorial Note: Use all-caps for `label-md` and `headline-sm` to lean into the utilitarian, "instruction manual" vibe.*
40	
41	---
42	
43	## 4. Elevation & Depth
44	In this system, "Elevation" does not exist in the 3D sense. We use **2D Offset** or **Tonal Layering**.
45	
46	- **The Hard-Shadow Principle:** Traditional soft shadows are prohibited. To elevate a card or button, use a **Hard Offset Shadow**: a solid block of `on_surface` color offset by 4px or 8px (e.g., `box-shadow: 4px 4px 0px 0px #000;`).
47	- **The Stacking Principle:** To show hierarchy, "nest" containers. A `surface-container-lowest` (#ffffff) card sitting on a `surface` (#f6f6f6) background, wrapped in a 2px black border, creates maximum focus.
48	- **Zero Radius:** Every element—buttons, inputs, cards, and tooltips—must have a `border-radius: 0px`. No exceptions.
49	
50	---
51	
52	## 5. Components
53	
54	### Buttons
55	- **Primary:** Background `primary`, text `on_primary`, 2px black border. On hover, offset the button by -2px and add a 4px hard black shadow to create a "lift" effect.
56	- **Secondary:** Background `secondary_container`, 2px black border.
57	- **Tertiary:** Background `transparent`, 2px black border, all-caps text.
58	
59	### Input Fields
60	- **Default:** Background `surface_container_lowest`, 2px black border.
61	- **Focus:** Border changes to `primary` (4px thick). Text does not shift.
62	- **Error:** Background `error_container`, 2px `error` border.
63	
64	### Cards & Lists
65	- **Cards:** Forbid divider lines within cards. Separate content using 2px black internal borders or distinct blocks of high-saturation color (e.g., a "Title" block in `primary_container` sitting atop a "Body" block in `surface_bright`).
66	- **Lists:** Each list item should be a distinct box with a 2px bottom border. Avoid "ghost" list items; every row must feel like a structural unit.
67	
68	### Code Editor Brackets (Signature Component)
69	- Use `tertiary_container` (Lime Green) as a high-contrast background for "Success" states in code execution blocks. The contrast between the lime green background and black text creates a "safety" or "industrial" warning look that is highly legible.
70	
71	---
72	
73	## 6. Do's and Don'ts
74	
75	### Do:
76	- **Embrace White Space:** Use large gaps (32px+) between bordered containers to let the "Raw" aesthetic breathe.
77	- **Use High-Saturation Accents:** Use `secondary` (Hot Pink) for notifications and `tertiary` (Lime) for successful code submissions.
78	- **Align to a Hard Grid:** Elements should feel like they are snapped to a rigid, mathematical grid.
79	
80	### Don't:
81	- **No Rounded Corners:** Never use border-radius. It breaks the "Utilitarian" vibe.
82	- **No Soft Shadows:** If it looks "fuzzy," it’s wrong. Shadows must be solid and offset.
83	- **No Gradients or Glassmorphism:** This system is about honesty; don't hide the background. Use solid fills only.
84	- **No 1px Lines:** They look "weak" in a Brutalist context. Stick to 2px or 4px for all structural strokes.

file.content:
# Design System Document: Competitive Brutalism

## 1. Overview & Creative North Star
### Creative North Star: "The Raw Compiler"
This design system rejects the "soft-and-safe" corporate aesthetic in favor of a raw, honest, and high-velocity environment tailored for competitive programmers. It is inspired by the terminal, the blueprint, and the brutalist architecture of early computing—stripped of unnecessary decoration to reveal the logic beneath.

We move beyond standard templates by using **Intentional Asymmetry** and **Rigid Logic**. The layout should feel like a living code editor: hyper-functional, high-contrast, and unapologetically bold. We break the "template" look by treating the screen as a grid of physical containers where information density is a feature, not a bug.

---

## 2. Colors & Surface Logic
The palette is built on a foundation of "Light Mode Terminal" (#f6f6f6) punctuated by high-voltage neon accents.

### Color Tokens
- **Primary (Electric Blue):** `#2752c7` — The color of action and logic.
- **Secondary (Hot Pink):** `#b60055` — Used for critical highlights and aggressive CTA states.
- **Tertiary (Lime Green):** `#106b00` — Represents "Success," "Passed," and "Optimized."
- **Background:** `#f6f6f6` — A clean, clinical stage for high-contrast elements.

### The "Heavy-Stroke" Rule
Unlike traditional systems that hide structure, this system celebrates it. **Prohibit 1px solid borders.** All structural boundaries must be defined by **2px to 4px solid black strokes** (`on_primary_fixed`). If a section doesn't have a border, it must be defined by a hard shift in background color using the Surface Hierarchy.

### Surface Hierarchy & Nesting
Depth is not simulated through light; it is earned through stacking.
- **Surface:** The base layer.
- **Surface-Container-Low (`#f1f1f1`):** For grouping secondary information.
- **Surface-Container-Highest (`#dddddd`):** Used for inactive code blocks or "sunken" UI elements.
- **The "No-Gradient" Rule:** Gradients are strictly forbidden. Use flat, high-saturation fills to maintain a "printed" or "poster" aesthetic.

---

## 3. Typography
The typography strategy creates a tension between the expressive "Display" layer and the clinical "Functional" layer.

- **Display & Headlines (Space Grotesk):** A heavy, wide sans-serif that feels engineered. Use `display-lg` (3.5rem) for rankings and hero moments. These should always be bold or extra-bold to match the 4px borders.
- **Body & Code (Inter):** While the headings are loud, the body text is quiet. Inter provides the legibility required for reading complex code logic.
- **Labels (Space Grotesk):** Used for metadata, tags, and "System" status updates.

*Editorial Note: Use all-caps for `label-md` and `headline-sm` to lean into the utilitarian, "instruction manual" vibe.*

---

## 4. Elevation & Depth
In this system, "Elevation" does not exist in the 3D sense. We use **2D Offset** or **Tonal Layering**.

- **The Hard-Shadow Principle:** Traditional soft shadows are prohibited. To elevate a card or button, use a **Hard Offset Shadow**: a solid block of `on_surface` color offset by 4px or 8px (e.g., `box-shadow: 4px 4px 0px 0px #000;`).
- **The Stacking Principle:** To show hierarchy, "nest" containers. A `surface-container-lowest` (#ffffff) card sitting on a `surface` (#f6f6f6) background, wrapped in a 2px black border, creates maximum focus.
- **Zero Radius:** Every element—buttons, inputs, cards, and tooltips—must have a `border-radius: 0px`. No exceptions.

---

## 5. Components

### Buttons
- **Primary:** Background `primary`, text `on_primary`, 2px black border. On hover, offset the button by -2px and add a 4px hard black shadow to create a "lift" effect.
- **Secondary:** Background `secondary_container`, 2px black border.
- **Tertiary:** Background `transparent`, 2px black border, all-caps text.

### Input Fields
- **Default:** Background `surface_container_lowest`, 2px black border.
- **Focus:** Border changes to `primary` (4px thick). Text does not shift.
- **Error:** Background `error_container`, 2px `error` border.

### Cards & Lists
- **Cards:** Forbid divider lines within cards. Separate content using 2px black internal borders or distinct blocks of high-saturation color (e.g., a "Title" block in `primary_container` sitting atop a "Body" block in `surface_bright`).
- **Lists:** Each list item should be a distinct box with a 2px bottom border. Avoid "ghost" list items; every row must feel like a structural unit.

### Code Editor Brackets (Signature Component)
- Use `tertiary_container` (Lime Green) as a high-contrast background for "Success" states in code execution blocks. The contrast between the lime green background and black text creates a "safety" or "industrial" warning look that is highly legible.

---

## 6. Do's and Don'ts

### Do:
- **Embrace White Space:** Use large gaps (32px+) between bordered containers to let the "Raw" aesthetic breathe.
- **Use High-Saturation Accents:** Use `secondary` (Hot Pink) for notifications and `tertiary` (Lime) for successful code submissions.
- **Align to a Hard Grid:** Elements should feel like they are snapped to a rigid, mathematical grid.

### Don't:
- **No Rounded Corners:** Never use border-radius. It breaks the "Utilitarian" vibe.
- **No Soft Shadows:** If it looks "fuzzy," it’s wrong. Shadows must be solid and offset.
- **No Gradients or Glassmorphism:** This system is about honesty; don't hide the background. Use solid fills only.
- **No 1px Lines:** They look "weak" in a Brutalist context. Stick to 2px or 4px for all structural strokes.

toolUseResult.content:
# Lab 2 – ASP.NET MVC Web App (Competitive Brutalism Design)

## Context

Lab 2 extends the existing WebIde project by adding an ASP.NET MVC web layer. The domain model
(`WebIde.Model`) and seed data (`WebIde.Console/Program.cs`) are complete from Lab 1. A Stitch design
kit was provided (`stitch_sharp_logic_arena.zip`, extracted to `stitch_sharp_logic_arena/`) with 4
Tailwind-based HTML mockups and a full design system document (`monoraw_hyper/DESIGN.md`).

**Design system: "Competitive Brutalism"**
- Background `#f6f6f6`, on-surface `#2f2f2f` — **light mode, not dark**
- Electric Blue `#2752c7`, Hot Pink `#b60055`, Lime Green `#106b00` accents
- Hard offset shadows: `box-shadow: 4px 4px 0px 0px #000` — no soft shadows
- Zero `border-radius` everywhere; 2–4px solid black borders on all structural elements
- **Space Grotesk** (headlines, labels, nav) + **Inter** (body, code)
- ALL-CAPS for labels, nav items, and headings — utilitarian "instruction manual" tone
- **Tailwind CSS (CDN)** — not Bootstrap; custom token config matches Stitch color map
- Material Symbols Outlined icons

**Requirements:**
- Custom Claude UX sub-agent (`.claude/agents/ux-designer.md`) committed to Git; uses the built-in `frontend-design` skill as its base with Competitive Brutalism style rules added
- Main agent spawns UX sub-agent when generating UI — log file as proof
- Mock repositories (static data from Lab 1) served via dependency injection
- Index + Details pages for every entity
- 4 Stitch-designed pages implemented faithfully as Razor views
- Complete navigation (top nav, left sidebar on inner pages, list → detail links, breadcrumbs)

---

## Stitch Pages → MVC Route Mapping

| Stitch file | Controller/Action | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `homepage/code.html` | `Home/Index` | Hero + bento grid (custom home page requirement) |
| `problems_list/code.html` | `Problem/Index` | Sidebar + filter panel + problems table |
| `leaderboard/code.html` | `Leaderboard/Index` | Podium top-3 + ranked table (User data sorted by score) |
| `solve_workspace/code.html` | `Problem/Details/{id}` | Split: problem description left, execution result right |

---

## Project Layout (new files)

```
.claude/agents/ux-designer.md            ← UX sub-agent instruction file (committed)

WebIde.Web/
  WebIde.Web.csproj                      ← .NET 10 MVC; references WebIde.Model
  Program.cs                             ← DI registrations (6 repos as Singleton), routing
  Repositories/
    MockData.cs                          ← Complete static seed graph (ported from Console)
    ProblemRepository.cs                 ← GetAll(), GetById()
    UserRepository.cs
    OrganizationRepository.cs
    ProblemSetRepository.cs
    SubmissionRepository.cs
    TagRepository.cs
  Controllers/
    HomeController.cs                    ← Index (aggregated stats for hero/bento)
    ProblemController.cs                 ← Index + Details
    LeaderboardController.cs             ← Index (users ranked by Accepted submissions)
    UserController.cs                    ← Index + Details
    OrganizationController.cs            ← Index + Details
    ProblemSetController.cs              ← Index + Details
    SubmissionController.cs              ← Index + Details
    TagController.cs                     ← Index + Details
  Views/
    Shared/
      _Layout.cshtml                     ← Competitive Brutalism: top nav, footer
      _Sidebar.cshtml                    ← Left sidebar partial (inner pages only)
      _ViewImports.cshtml
      _ViewStart.cshtml
    Home/Index.cshtml                    ← Stitch homepage (hero + bento)
    Problem/
      Index.cshtml                       ← Stitch problems_list (sidebar + table)
      Details.cshtml                     ← Stitch solve_workspace (description + results)
    Leaderboard/Index.cshtml             ← Stitch leaderboard (podium + ranked table)
    User/Index.cshtml + Details.cshtml
    Organization/Index.cshtml + Details.cshtml
    ProblemSet/Index.cshtml + Details.cshtml
    Submission/Index.cshtml + Details.cshtml
    Tag/Index.cshtml + Details.cshtml
  wwwroot/
    css/site.css                         ← Tailwind-CDN + Competitive Brutalism custom CSS

lab-2/
  ux-agent-invocation.log               ← Proof that UX sub-agent was spawned

WebIde.slnx                             ← Add WebIde.Web project reference
```

---

## Task Breakdown

### Task 1 — UX Sub-agent instruction file
**File:** `.claude/agents/ux-designer.md`

A Claude Code sub-agent that:
- Wraps the built-in `frontend-design` skill
- Style override: Competitive Brutalism rules (zero radius, 2–4px borders, hard offset shadows, Space Grotesk headlines, ALL-CAPS labels, `#f6f6f6` surface, `#2752c7/#b60055/#106b00` accents)
- Component rules: difficulty badge pills (lime/blue/pink per Easy/Medium/Hard), status chips for submissions, code block styling matching solve_workspace dark area
- Scoped tools: Read, Edit, Write, Glob, Grep

---

### Task 2 — Create `WebIde.Web` ASP.NET MVC project
**Files:** `WebIde.Web/WebIde.Web.csproj`, `Program.cs`, `WebIde.slnx`

- Target `.NET 10`; reference `WebIde.Model`
- `Program.cs`: register 6 repos as `AddSingleton<T>()`, default route `{controller=Home}/{action=Index}/{id?}`
- No Bootstrap — Tailwind loaded via CDN script in `_Layout.cshtml`

---

### Task 3 — Mock repositories + expanded seed data
**Files:** `Repositories/MockData.cs` + 6 repo files

`MockData.cs` — static sealed class holding the full seed graph. **Expand** beyond Lab 1 minimal data:
- 3 problems (from Lab 1) + 2 extra problems (Medium/Hard variety)
- 4 tags (from Lab 1)
- 3 users (from Lab 1) + 1 extra user (Admin role)
- **2 orgs** (original FER Algorithms + new "Open Source Club")
- **2 problem sets** (original Week 1 + new "Advanced Algorithms")
- 8 submissions (from Lab 1, all linked)

Each repository: `GetAll()` + `GetById(int id)`.

`TestCase` and `ExecutionResult` accessed via parent — no standalone repos.

---

### Task 4 — Shared layout + CSS
**Files:** `Views/Shared/_Layout.cshtml`, `_Sidebar.cshtml`, `_ViewImports.cshtml`, `_ViewStart.cshtml`, `wwwroot/css/site.css`

`_Layout.cshtml`:
- Loads Tailwind CDN + tailwind.config matching exact Stitch color tokens
- Loads Space Grotesk + Inter + Material Symbols from Google Fonts
- Fixed top nav: `CODE_COMPILER` italic logo + nav links (PROBLEMS, LEADERBOARD, CONTESTS) + terminal/notifications icons + SUBMIT button
- All links use `asp-controller`/`asp-action` Tag Helpers
- `@RenderBody()` main content area
- Fixed footer: black bar with copyright + DOCS/API/STATUS/LEGAL links

`_Sidebar.cshtml`:
- Partial injected on inner pages (Problems, Leaderboard, User, etc.)
- User identity block at top (hardcoded mock user: ROOT_USER / Rank #1)
- Nav items: DASHBOARD, PRACTICE (active on Problem pages), COMPETE, STATS, SETTINGS
- UPGRADE_PRO button (Hot Pink, hard shadow)

`site.css`:
- `.hard-shadow`, `.brutal-border`, `.brutal-border-heavy` utility classes
- Hard-shadow hover lift effect (`:active` translate + shadow-none)
- Difficulty badge color classes

---

### Task 5 — Home page (Hero + Bento)
**Files:** `HomeController.cs`, `Views/Home/Index.cshtml`

Controller builds a ViewModel aggregating:
- Total problems, users, submissions
- Acceptance rate across all submissions
- Top 3 users by Accepted submission count (for Leaderboard bento card)

View: **Faithful port of `homepage/code.html`**:
- Hero section: "COMPETE. CODE. CONQUER." + description + CTA buttons → link to Problem/Index
- Bento grid: Problems card (8-col) + Leaderboard mini (4-col) + full-width feature + stats bars + upgrade card
- Dynamic data injected: real problem count, real user leaderboard top 3, real submission stats

---

### Task 6 — Problem pages
**Files:** `ProblemController.cs`, `Views/Problem/Index.cshtml`, `Views/Problem/Details.cshtml`

**Index** — **Faithful port of `problems_list/code.html`**:
- Sidebar via `@await Html.PartialAsync("_Sidebar")`
- Stats cards: total solved (of 3 problems), success rate, global rank
- Filter panel (Difficulty checkboxes, tag chips) — static (no server-side filtering needed, visual only)
- Problems table: solved status icon, title+tags (link to Details), difficulty badge pill, acceptance %, SOLVE button
- Breadcrumb: Home > PROBLEMS

**Details** — **Faithful port of `solve_workspace/code.html`**:
- Left pane: problem title, difficulty badge, time/memory limits, full description, sample test cases
- Right section: tab bar (CONSOLE | TEST_CASES | SUBMISSIONS)
  - CONSOLE tab: execution result stdout/stderr (from most recent submission)
  - TEST_CASES tab: sample test cases table
  - SUBMISSIONS tab: all submissions for this problem

---

### Task 7 — Leaderboard page
**File:** `LeaderboardController.cs`, `Views/Leaderboard/Index.cshtml`

Controller: sort all users by Accepted submission count desc → pass list.

View — **Faithful port of `leaderboard/code.html`**:
- Sidebar
- Header: "LEADERBOARD" with `border-l-8 border-primary`
- Podium: top 3 users in asymmetric bento (tertiary-container for #1, primary-container for #2, secondary-container for #3)
- Main ranked table: all remaining users (rank #, username, problems solved, score)
- Current user row highlighted with `text-primary underline` + "YOU" badge
- Stats cards at bottom: active competitors, lines committed, avg execution time

---

### Task 8 — User pages
**Files:** `UserController.cs`, `Views/User/Index.cshtml`, `Views/User/Details.cshtml`

Index: User list — Display name (link to Details), username, role badge, registered date, submission count, solve rate. Follows Competitive Brutalism table style.

Details: Profile header (initial avatar box, display name, role badge), submission history table (problem link, status chip, score, time, submitted), solve rate stat card.

---

### Task 9 — Organization pages
**Files:** `OrganizationController.cs`, `Views/Organization/Index.cshtml`, `Views/Organization/Details.cshtml`

Index: Org cards (hard shadow) — name, description snippet, member count badge, problem set count.
Details: Full description, members list (linked to User/Details), problem sets list (linked to ProblemSet/Details).

---

### Task 10 — ProblemSet pages
**Files:** `ProblemSetController.cs`, `Views/ProblemSet/Index.cshtml`, `Views/ProblemSet/Details.cshtml`

Index: Set list — title (link), org name, public/private pill, problem count, created date.
Details: Title, description, public badge, org link, ordered problems table (linked to Problem/Details).

---

### Task 11 — Submission pages
**Files:** `SubmissionController.cs`, `Views/Submission/Index.cshtml`, `Views/Submission/Details.cshtml`

Index: All submissions — user link, problem link, language badge, status chip (color-coded: tertiary=Accepted, error=WrongAnswer/CompileError, primary=TLE/MLE), score, wall time, memory.
Details: User + problem links; status; score; source code block (dark `#0e0e0e` background matching solve_workspace editor area); ExecutionResult section (stdout, stderr, exit code, timed out, memory exceeded).

---

### Task 12 — Tag pages
**Files:** `TagController.cs`, `Views/Tag/Index.cshtml`, `Views/Tag/Details.cshtml`

Index: Tag list with problem count badges in Competitive Brutalism pill style.
Details: Tag name header; problems tagged (table linking to Problem/Details).

---

### Task 13 — Agent invocation log
**File:** `lab-2/ux-agent-invocation.log`

Structured log entry recording UX sub-agent spawn: timestamp, task description, which views were generated, output summary. Created when UX sub-agent is actually invoked during layout/CSS and Stitch view implementation tasks.

---

## Navigation completeness matrix

| Source | Destination |
|---|---|
| Top nav | Home, Problem/Index, Leaderboard/Index |
| Sidebar | Dashboard (Home), Problem/Index, Leaderboard, User/Index, Tag/Index |
| Problem/Index row | Problem/Details/{id} |
| Problem/Details | Tag/Details (per tag chip), User/Details (via submission rows) |
| Leaderboard row | User/Details/{id} |
| User/Details | Submission/Details, Problem/Details |
| Submission/Index row | Submission/Details, User/Details, Problem/Details |
| Organization/Details | User/Details (members), ProblemSet/Details |
| ProblemSet/Details | Problem/Details, Organization/Details |
| Tag/Details | Problem/Details |

All inner pages include breadcrumbs via a `@section` or inline breadcrumb bar.

---

## Design implementation notes

- **Tailwind config** in `_Layout.cshtml` must include the exact color token map from the Stitch HTML files (already extracted above)
- **`border-radius: 0px` override**: Set `DEFAULT`, `lg`, `xl` all to `0px` in Tailwind config; `full` stays `9999px` for pill shapes only
- **No Tailwind purge in CDN mode** — all utility classes available without build step, appropriate for Razor views
- The solve_workspace editor code area uses an inline dark `bg-[#0e0e0e]` region — this is the only dark element on an otherwise light page

---

## Verification

1. `dotnet build WebIde.Web` → no errors
2. `dotnet run --project WebIde.Web` → app starts on `localhost:5xxx`
3. Navigate all routes: `/`, `/Problem`, `/Problem/Details/1–3`, `/Leaderboard`, `/User`, `/User/Details/1–3`, `/Organization`, `/Organization/Details/1–2`, `/ProblemSet`, `/ProblemSet/Details/1–2`, `/Submission`, `/Submission/Details/1–8`, `/Tag`, `/Tag/Details/1–4`
4. Visual check: all pages match Competitive Brutalism (hard shadows, zero radius, Space Grotesk headlines, no soft shadows/gradients)
5. The 4 Stitch-designed pages (Home, Problem/Index, Problem/Details, Leaderboard) closely match their `code.html` counterparts
6. `.claude/agents/ux-designer.md` exists and is committed
7. `lab-2/ux-agent-invocation.log` shows sub-agent was spawned
8. All nav links and cross-entity links resolve (no 404s)

# Lab 2 – ASP.NET MVC Web App (Competitive Brutalism Design)

## Context

Lab 2 extends the existing WebIde project by adding an ASP.NET MVC web layer. The domain model
(`WebIde.Model`) and seed data (`WebIde.Console/Program.cs`) are complete from Lab 1. A Stitch design
kit was provided (`stitch_sharp_logic_arena.zip`, extracted to `stitch_sharp_logic_arena/`) with 4
Tailwind-based HTML mockups and a full design system document (`monoraw_hyper/DESIGN.md`).

**Design system: "Competitive Brutalism"**
- Background `#f6f6f6`, on-surface `#2f2f2f` — **light mode, not dark**
- Electric Blue `#2752c7`, Hot Pink `#b60055`, Lime Green `#106b00` accents
- Hard offset shadows: `box-shadow: 4px 4px 0px 0px #000` — no soft shadows
- Zero `border-radius` everywhere; 2–4px solid black borders on all structural elements
- **Space Grotesk** (headlines, labels, nav) + **Inter** (body, code)
- ALL-CAPS for labels, nav items, and headings — utilitarian "instruction manual" tone
- **Tailwind CSS (CDN)** — not Bootstrap; custom token config matches Stitch color map
- Material Symbols Outlined icons

**Requirements:**
- Custom Claude UX sub-agent (`.claude/agents/ux-designer.md`) committed to Git; uses the built-in `frontend-design` skill as its base with Competitive Brutalism style rules added
- Main agent spawns UX sub-agent when generating UI — log file as proof
- Mock repositories (static data from Lab 1) served via dependency injection
- Index + Details pages for every entity
- 4 Stitch-designed pages implemented faithfully as Razor views
- Complete navigation (top nav, left sidebar on inner pages, list → detail links, breadcrumbs)

---

## Stitch Pages → MVC Route Mapping

| Stitch file | Controller/Action | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `homepage/code.html` | `Home/Index` | Hero + bento grid (custom home page requirement) |
| `problems_list/code.html` | `Problem/Index` | Sidebar + filter panel + problems table |
| `leaderboard/code.html` | `Leaderboard/Index` | Podium top-3 + ranked table (User data sorted by score) |
| `solve_workspace/code.html` | `Problem/Details/{id}` | Split: problem description left, execution result right |

---

## Project Layout (new files)

```
.claude/agents/ux-designer.md            ← UX sub-agent instruction file (committed)

WebIde.Web/
  WebIde.Web.csproj                      ← .NET 10 MVC; references WebIde.Model
  Program.cs                             ← DI registrations (6 repos as Singleton), routing
  Repositories/
    MockData.cs                          ← Complete static seed graph (ported from Console)
    ProblemRepository.cs                 ← GetAll(), GetById()
    UserRepository.cs
    OrganizationRepository.cs
    ProblemSetRepository.cs
    SubmissionRepository.cs
    TagRepository.cs
  Controllers/
    HomeController.cs                    ← Index (aggregated stats for hero/bento)
    ProblemController.cs                 ← Index + Details
    LeaderboardController.cs             ← Index (users ranked by Accepted submissions)
    UserController.cs                    ← Index + Details
    OrganizationController.cs            ← Index + Details
    ProblemSetController.cs              ← Index + Details
    SubmissionController.cs              ← Index + Details
    TagController.cs                     ← Index + Details
  Views/
    Shared/
      _Layout.cshtml                     ← Competitive Brutalism: top nav, footer
      _Sidebar.cshtml                    ← Left sidebar partial (inner pages only)
      _ViewImports.cshtml
      _ViewStart.cshtml
    Home/Index.cshtml                    ← Stitch homepage (hero + bento)
    Problem/
      Index.cshtml                       ← Stitch problems_list (sidebar + table)
      Details.cshtml                     ← Stitch solve_workspace (description + results)
    Leaderboard/Index.cshtml             ← Stitch leaderboard (podium + ranked table)
    User/Index.cshtml + Details.cshtml
    Organization/Index.cshtml + Details.cshtml
    ProblemSet/Index.cshtml + Details.cshtml
    Submission/Index.cshtml + Details.cshtml
    Tag/Index.cshtml + Details.cshtml
  wwwroot/
    css/site.css                         ← Tailwind-CDN + Competitive Brutalism custom CSS

lab-2/
  ux-agent-invocation.log               ← Proof that UX sub-agent was spawned

WebIde.slnx                             ← Add WebIde.Web project reference
```

---

## Task Breakdown

### Task 1 — UX Sub-agent instruction file
**File:** `.claude/agents/ux-designer.md`

A Claude Code sub-agent that:
- Wraps the built-in `frontend-design` skill
- Style override: Competitive Brutalism rules (zero radius, 2–4px borders, hard offset shadows, Space Grotesk headlines, ALL-CAPS labels, `#f6f6f6` surface, `#2752c7/#b60055/#106b00` accents)
- Component rules: difficulty badge pills (lime/blue/pink per Easy/Medium/Hard), status chips for submissions, code block styling matching solve_workspace dark area
- Scoped tools: Read, Edit, Write, Glob, Grep

---

### Task 2 — Create `WebIde.Web` ASP.NET MVC project
**Files:** `WebIde.Web/WebIde.Web.csproj`, `Program.cs`, `WebIde.slnx`

- Target `.NET 10`; reference `WebIde.Model`
- `Program.cs`: register 6 repos as `AddSingleton<T>()`, default route `{controller=Home}/{action=Index}/{id?}`
- No Bootstrap — Tailwind loaded via CDN script in `_Layout.cshtml`

---

### Task 3 — Mock repositories + expanded seed data
**Files:** `Repositories/MockData.cs` + 6 repo files

`MockData.cs` — static sealed class holding the full seed graph. **Expand** beyond Lab 1 minimal data:
- 3 problems (from Lab 1) + 2 extra problems (Medium/Hard variety)
- 4 tags (from Lab 1)
- 3 users (from Lab 1) + 1 extra user (Admin role)
- **2 orgs** (original FER Algorithms + new "Open Source Club")
- **2 problem sets** (original Week 1 + new "Advanced Algorithms")
- 8 submissions (from Lab 1, all linked)

Each repository: `GetAll()` + `GetById(int id)`.

`TestCase` and `ExecutionResult` accessed via parent — no standalone repos.

---

### Task 4 — Shared layout + CSS
**Files:** `Views/Shared/_Layout.cshtml`, `_Sidebar.cshtml`, `_ViewImports.cshtml`, `_ViewStart.cshtml`, `wwwroot/css/site.css`

`_Layout.cshtml`:
- Loads Tailwind CDN + tailwind.config matching exact Stitch color tokens
- Loads Space Grotesk + Inter + Material Symbols from Google Fonts
- Fixed top nav: `CODE_COMPILER` italic logo + nav links (PROBLEMS, LEADERBOARD, CONTESTS) + terminal/notifications icons + SUBMIT button
- All links use `asp-controller`/`asp-action` Tag Helpers
- `@RenderBody()` main content area
- Fixed footer: black bar with copyright + DOCS/API/STATUS/LEGAL links

`_Sidebar.cshtml`:
- Partial injected on inner pages (Problems, Leaderboard, User, etc.)
- User identity block at top (hardcoded mock user: ROOT_USER / Rank #1)
- Nav items: DASHBOARD, PRACTICE (active on Problem pages), COMPETE, STATS, SETTINGS
- UPGRADE_PRO button (Hot Pink, hard shadow)

`site.css`:
- `.hard-shadow`, `.brutal-border`, `.brutal-border-heavy` utility classes
- Hard-shadow hover lift effect (`:active` translate + shadow-none)
- Difficulty badge color classes

---

### Task 5 — Home page (Hero + Bento)
**Files:** `HomeController.cs`, `Views/Home/Index.cshtml`

Controller builds a ViewModel aggregating:
- Total problems, users, submissions
- Acceptance rate across all submissions
- Top 3 users by Accepted submission count (for Leaderboard bento card)

View: **Faithful port of `homepage/code.html`**:
- Hero section: "COMPETE. CODE. CONQUER." + description + CTA buttons → link to Problem/Index
- Bento grid: Problems card (8-col) + Leaderboard mini (4-col) + full-width feature + stats bars + upgrade card
- Dynamic data injected: real problem count, real user leaderboard top 3, real submission stats

---

### Task 6 — Problem pages
**Files:** `ProblemController.cs`, `Views/Problem/Index.cshtml`, `Views/Problem/Details.cshtml`

**Index** — **Faithful port of `problems_list/code.html`**:
- Sidebar via `@await Html.PartialAsync("_Sidebar")`
- Stats cards: total solved (of 3 problems), success rate, global rank
- Filter panel (Difficulty checkboxes, tag chips) — static (no server-side filtering needed, visual only)
- Problems table: solved status icon, title+tags (link to Details), difficulty badge pill, acceptance %, SOLVE button
- Breadcrumb: Home > PROBLEMS

**Details** — **Faithful port of `solve_workspace/code.html`**:
- Left pane: problem title, difficulty badge, time/memory limits, full description, sample test cases
- Right section: tab bar (CONSOLE | TEST_CASES | SUBMISSIONS)
  - CONSOLE tab: execution result stdout/stderr (from most recent submission)
  - TEST_CASES tab: sample test cases table
  - SUBMISSIONS tab: all submissions for this problem

---

### Task 7 — Leaderboard page
**File:** `LeaderboardController.cs`, `Views/Leaderboard/Index.cshtml`

Controller: sort all users by Accepted submission count desc → pass list.

View — **Faithful port of `leaderboard/code.html`**:
- Sidebar
- Header: "LEADERBOARD" with `border-l-8 border-primary`
- Podium: top 3 users in asymmetric bento (tertiary-container for #1, primary-container for #2, secondary-container for #3)
- Main ranked table: all remaining users (rank #, username, problems solved, score)
- Current user row highlighted with `text-primary underline` + "YOU" badge
- Stats cards at bottom: active competitors, lines committed, avg execution time

---

### Task 8 — User pages
**Files:** `UserController.cs`, `Views/User/Index.cshtml`, `Views/User/Details.cshtml`

Index: User list — Display name (link to Details), username, role badge, registered date, submission count, solve rate. Follows Competitive Brutalism table style.

Details: Profile header (initial avatar box, display name, role badge), submission history table (problem link, status chip, score, time, submitted), solve rate stat card.

---

### Task 9 — Organization pages
**Files:** `OrganizationController.cs`, `Views/Organization/Index.cshtml`, `Views/Organization/Details.cshtml`

Index: Org cards (hard shadow) — name, description snippet, member count badge, problem set count.
Details: Full description, members list (linked to User/Details), problem sets list (linked to ProblemSet/Details).

---

### Task 10 — ProblemSet pages
**Files:** `ProblemSetController.cs`, `Views/ProblemSet/Index.cshtml`, `Views/ProblemSet/Details.cshtml`

Index: Set list — title (link), org name, public/private pill, problem count, created date.
Details: Title, description, public badge, org link, ordered problems table (linked to Problem/Details).

---

### Task 11 — Submission pages
**Files:** `SubmissionController.cs`, `Views/Submission/Index.cshtml`, `Views/Submission/Details.cshtml`

Index: All submissions — user link, problem link, language badge, status chip (color-coded: tertiary=Accepted, error=WrongAnswer/CompileError, primary=TLE/MLE), score, wall time, memory.
Details: User + problem links; status; score; source code block (dark `#0e0e0e` background matching solve_workspace editor area); ExecutionResult section (stdout, stderr, exit code, timed out, memory exceeded).

---

### Task 12 — Tag pages
**Files:** `TagController.cs`, `Views/Tag/Index.cshtml`, `Views/Tag/Details.cshtml`

Index: Tag list with problem count badges in Competitive Brutalism pill style.
Details: Tag name header; problems tagged (table linking to Problem/Details).

---

### Task 13 — Agent invocation log
**File:** `lab-2/ux-agent-invocation.log`

Structured log entry recording UX sub-agent spawn: timestamp, task description, which views were generated, output summary. Created when UX sub-agent is actually invoked during layout/CSS and Stitch view implementation tasks.

---

## Navigation completeness matrix

| Source | Destination |
|---|---|
| Top nav | Home, Problem/Index, Leaderboard/Index |
| Sidebar | Dashboard (Home), Problem/Index, Leaderboard, User/Index, Tag/Index |
| Problem/Index row | Problem/Details/{id} |
| Problem/Details | Tag/Details (per tag chip), User/Details (via submission rows) |
| Leaderboard row | User/Details/{id} |
| User/Details | Submission/Details, Problem/Details |
| Submission/Index row | Submission/Details, User/Details, Problem/Details |
| Organization/Details | User/Details (members), ProblemSet/Details |
| ProblemSet/Details | Problem/Details, Organization/Details |
| Tag/Details | Problem/Details |

All inner pages include breadcrumbs via a `@section` or inline breadcrumb bar.

---

## Design implementation notes

- **Tailwind config** in `_Layout.cshtml` must include the exact color token map from the Stitch HTML files (already extracted above)
- **`border-radius: 0px` override**: Set `DEFAULT`, `lg`, `xl` all to `0px` in Tailwind config; `full` stays `9999px` for pill shapes only
- **No Tailwind purge in CDN mode** — all utility classes available without build step, appropriate for Razor views
- The solve_workspace editor code area uses an inline dark `bg-[#0e0e0e]` region — this is the only dark element on an otherwise light page

---

## Verification

1. `dotnet build WebIde.Web` → no errors
2. `dotnet run --project WebIde.Web` → app starts on `localhost:5xxx`
3. Navigate all routes: `/`, `/Problem`, `/Problem/Details/1–3`, `/Leaderboard`, `/User`, `/User/Details/1–3`, `/Organization`, `/Organization/Details/1–2`, `/ProblemSet`, `/ProblemSet/Details/1–2`, `/Submission`, `/Submission/Details/1–8`, `/Tag`, `/Tag/Details/1–4`
4. Visual check: all pages match Competitive Brutalism (hard shadows, zero radius, Space Grotesk headlines, no soft shadows/gradients)
5. The 4 Stitch-designed pages (Home, Problem/Index, Problem/Details, Leaderboard) closely match their `code.html` counterparts
6. `.claude/agents/ux-designer.md` exists and is committed
7. `lab-2/ux-agent-invocation.log` shows sub-agent was spawned
8. All nav links and cross-entity links resolve (no 404s)

# Lab 2 – ASP.NET MVC Web App (Competitive Brutalism Design)

## Context

Lab 2 extends the existing WebIde project by adding an ASP.NET MVC web layer. The domain model
(`WebIde.Model`) and seed data (`WebIde.Console/Program.cs`) are complete from Lab 1. A Stitch design
kit was provided (`stitch_sharp_logic_arena.zip`, extracted to `stitch_sharp_logic_arena/`) with 4
Tailwind-based HTML mockups and a full design system document (`monoraw_hyper/DESIGN.md`).

**Design system: "Competitive Brutalism"**
- Background `#f6f6f6`, on-surface `#2f2f2f` — **light mode, not dark**
- Electric Blue `#2752c7`, Hot Pink `#b60055`, Lime Green `#106b00` accents
- Hard offset shadows: `box-shadow: 4px 4px 0px 0px #000` — no soft shadows
- Zero `border-radius` everywhere; 2–4px solid black borders on all structural elements
- **Space Grotesk** (headlines, labels, nav) + **Inter** (body, code)
- ALL-CAPS for labels, nav items, and headings — utilitarian "instruction manual" tone
- **Tailwind CSS (CDN)** — not Bootstrap; custom token config matches Stitch color map
- Material Symbols Outlined icons

**Requirements:**
- Custom Claude UX sub-agent (`.claude/agents/ux-designer.md`) committed to Git; uses the built-in `frontend-design` skill as its base with Competitive Brutalism style rules added
- Main agent spawns UX sub-agent when generating UI — log file as proof
- Mock repositories (static data from Lab 1) served via dependency injection
- Index + Details pages for every entity
- 4 Stitch-designed pages implemented faithfully as Razor views
- Complete navigation (top nav, left sidebar on inner pages, list → detail links, breadcrumbs)

---

| `leaderboard/code.html` | `Leaderboard/Index` | Podium top-3 + ranked table (User data sorted by score) |
| `solve_workspace/code.html` | `Problem/Details/{id}` | Split: problem description left, execution result right |

---

## Project Layout (new files)

```
.claude/agents/ux-designer.md            ← UX sub-agent instruction file (committed)

WebIde.Web/
  WebIde.Web.csproj                      ← .NET 10 MVC; references WebIde.Model
  Program.cs                             ← DI registrations (6 repos as Singleton), routing
  Repositories/
    MockData.cs                          ← Complete static seed graph (ported from Console)
    ProblemRepository.cs                 ← GetAll(), GetById()
    UserRepository.cs
    OrganizationRepository.cs
    ProblemSetRepository.cs
    SubmissionRepository.cs
    TagRepository.cs
  Controllers/
    HomeController.cs                    ← Index (aggregated stats for hero/bento)
    ProblemController.cs                 ← Index + Details
    LeaderboardController.cs             ← Index (users ranked by Accepted submissions)
    UserController.cs                    ← Index + Details
    OrganizationController.cs            ← Index + Details
    ProblemSetController.cs              ← Index + Details
    SubmissionController.cs              ← Index + Details
    TagController.cs                     ← Index + Details
  Views/
    Shared/
      _Layout.cshtml                     ← Competitive Brutalism: top nav, footer
      _Sidebar.cshtml                    ← Left sidebar partial (inner pages only)
      _ViewImports.cshtml
      _ViewStart.cshtml
    Home/Index.cshtml                    ← Stitch homepage (hero + bento)
    Problem/
      Index.cshtml                       ← Stitch problems_list (sidebar + table)
      Details.cshtml                     ← Stitch solve_workspace (description + results)
    Leaderboard/Index.cshtml             ← Stitch leaderboard (podium + ranked table)
    User/Index.cshtml + Details.cshtml
    Organization/Index.cshtml + Details.cshtml
    ProblemSet/Index.cshtml + Details.cshtml
    Submission/Index.cshtml + Details.cshtml
    Tag/Index.cshtml + Details.cshtml
  wwwroot/
    css/site.css                         ← Tailwind-CDN + Competitive Brutalism custom CSS

lab-2/
  ux-agent-invocation.log               ← Proof that UX sub-agent was spawned

WebIde.slnx                             ← Add WebIde.Web project reference
```

---

## Task Breakdown

### Task 1 — UX Sub-agent instruction file
**File:** `.claude/agents/ux-designer.md`

A Claude Code sub-agent that:
- Wraps the built-in `frontend-design` skill
- Style override: Competitive Brutalism rules (zero radius, 2–4px borders, hard offset shadows, Space Grotesk headlines, ALL-CAPS labels, `#f6f6f6` surface, `#2752c7/#b60055/#106b00` accents)
- Component rules: difficulty badge pills (lime/blue/pink per Easy/Medium/Hard), status chips for submissions, code block styling matching solve_workspace dark area
- Scoped tools: Read, Edit, Write, Glob, Grep

---

### Task 2 — Create `WebIde.Web` ASP.NET MVC project
**Files:** `WebIde.Web/WebIde.Web.csproj`, `Program.cs`, `WebIde.slnx`

- Target `.NET 10`; reference `WebIde.Model`
- `Program.cs`: register 6 repos as `AddSingleton<T>()`, default route `{controller=Home}/{action=Index}/{id?}`
- No Bootstrap — Tailwind loaded via CDN script in `_Layout.cshtml`

---

### Task 3 — Mock repositories + expanded seed data
**Files:** `Repositories/MockData.cs` + 6 repo files

`MockData.cs` — static sealed class holding the full seed graph. **Expand** beyond Lab 1 minimal data:
- 3 problems (from Lab 1) + 2 extra problems (Medium/Hard variety)
- 4 tags (from Lab 1)
- 3 users (from Lab 1) + 1 extra user (Admin role)
- **2 orgs** (original FER Algorithms + new "Open Source Club")
- **2 problem sets** (original Week 1 + new "Advanced Algorithms")
- 8 submissions (from Lab 1, all linked)

Each repository: `GetAll()` + `GetById(int id)`.

`TestCase` and `ExecutionResult` accessed via parent — no standalone repos.

---

### Task 4 — Shared layout + CSS
**Files:** `Views/Shared/_Layout.cshtml`, `_Sidebar.cshtml`, `_ViewImports.cshtml`, `_ViewStart.cshtml`, `wwwroot/css/site.css`

`_Layout.cshtml`:
- Loads Tailwind CDN + tailwind.config matching exact Stitch color tokens
- Loads Space Grotesk + Inter + Material Symbols from Google Fonts
- Fixed top nav: `CODE_COMPILER` italic logo + nav links (PROBLEMS, LEADERBOARD, CONTESTS) + terminal/notifications icons + SUBMIT button
- All links use `asp-controller`/`asp-action` Tag Helpers
- `@RenderBody()` main content area
- Fixed footer: black bar with copyright + DOCS/API/STATUS/LEGAL links

`_Sidebar.cshtml`:
- Partial injected on inner pages (Problems, Leaderboard, User, etc.)
- User identity block at top (hardcoded mock user: ROOT_USER / Rank #1)
- Nav items: DASHBOARD, PRACTICE (active on Problem pages), COMPETE, STATS, SETTINGS
- UPGRADE_PRO button (Hot Pink, hard shadow)

`site.css`:
- `.hard-shadow`, `.brutal-border`, `.brutal-border-heavy` utility classes
- Hard-shadow hover lift effect (`:active` translate + shadow-none)
- Difficulty badge color classes

---

### Task 5 — Home page (Hero + Bento)
**Files:** `HomeController.cs`, `Views/Home/Index.cshtml`

Controller builds a ViewModel aggregating:
- Total problems, users, submissions
- Acceptance rate across all submissions
- Top 3 users by Accepted submission count (for Leaderboard bento card)

View: **Faithful port of `homepage/code.html`**:
- Hero section: "COMPETE. CODE. CONQUER." + description + CTA buttons → link to Problem/Index
- Bento grid: Problems card (8-col) + Leaderboard mini (4-col) + full-width feature + stats bars + upgrade card
- Dynamic data injected: real problem count, real user leaderboard top 3, real submission stats

---

### Task 6 — Problem pages
**Files:** `ProblemController.cs`, `Views/Problem/Index.cshtml`, `Views/Problem/Details.cshtml`

**Index** — **Faithful port of `problems_list/code.html`**:
- Sidebar via `@await Html.PartialAsync("_Sidebar")`
- Stats cards: total solved (of 3 problems), success rate, global rank
- Filter panel (Difficulty checkboxes, tag chips) — static (no server-side filtering needed, visual only)
- Problems table: solved status icon, title+tags (link to Details), difficulty badge pill, acceptance %, SOLVE button
- Breadcrumb: Home > PROBLEMS

**Details** — **Faithful port of `solve_workspace/code.html`**:
- Left pane: problem title, difficulty badge, time/memory limits, full description, sample test cases
- Right section: tab bar (CONSOLE | TEST_CASES | SUBMISSIONS)
  - CONSOLE tab: execution result stdout/stderr (from most recent submission)
  - TEST_CASES tab: sample test cases table
  - SUBMISSIONS tab: all submissions for this problem

---

### Task 7 — Leaderboard page
**File:** `LeaderboardController.cs`, `Views/Leaderboard/Index.cshtml`

Controller: sort all users by Accepted submission count desc → pass list.

View — **Faithful port of `leaderboard/code.html`**:
- Sidebar
- Header: "LEADERBOARD" with `border-l-8 border-primary`
- Podium: top 3 users in asymmetric bento (tertiary-container for #1, primary-container for #2, secondary-container for #3)
- Main ranked table: all remaining users (rank #, username, problems solved, score)
- Current user row highlighted with `text-primary underline` + "YOU" badge
- Stats cards at bottom: active competitors, lines committed, avg execution time

---

### Task 8 — User pages
**Files:** `UserController.cs`, `Views/User/Index.cshtml`, `Views/User/Details.cshtml`

Index: User list — Display name (link to Details), username, role badge, registered date, submission count, solve rate. Follows Competitive Brutalism table style.

Details: Profile header (initial avatar box, display name, role badge), submission history table (problem link, status chip, score, time, submitted), solve rate stat card.

---

### Task 9 — Organization pages
**Files:** `OrganizationController.cs`, `Views/Organization/Index.cshtml`, `Views/Organization/Details.cshtml`

Index: Org cards (hard shadow) — name, description snippet, member count badge, problem set count.
Details: Full description, members list (linked to User/Details), problem sets list (linked to ProblemSet/Details).

---

### Task 10 — ProblemSet pages
**Files:** `ProblemSetController.cs`, `Views/ProblemSet/Index.cshtml`, `Views/ProblemSet/Details.cshtml`

Index: Set list — title (link), org name, public/private pill, problem count, created date.
Details: Title, description, public badge, org link, ordered problems table (linked to Problem/Details).

---

### Task 11 — Submission pages
**Files:** `SubmissionController.cs`, `Views/Submission/Index.cshtml`, `Views/Submission/Details.cshtml`

Index: All submissions — user link, problem link, language badge, status chip (color-coded: tertiary=Accepted, error=WrongAnswer/CompileError, primary=TLE/MLE), score, wall time, memory.
Details: User + problem links; status; score; source code block (dark `#0e0e0e` background matching solve_workspace editor area); ExecutionResult section (stdout, stderr, exit code, timed out, memory exceeded).

---

### Task 12 — Tag pages
**Files:** `TagController.cs`, `Views/Tag/Index.cshtml`, `Views/Tag/Details.cshtml`

Index: Tag list with problem count badges in Competitive Brutalism pill style.
Details: Tag name header; problems tagged (table linking to Problem/Details).

---

### Task 13 — Agent invocation log
**File:** `lab-2/ux-agent-invocation.log`

Structured log entry recording UX sub-agent spawn: timestamp, task description, which views were generated, output summary. Created when UX sub-agent is actually invoked during layout/CSS and Stitch view implementation tasks.

---

## Navigation completeness matrix

| Source | Destination |
|---|---|
| Top nav | Home, Problem/Index, Leaderboard/Index |
| Sidebar | Dashboard (Home), Problem/Index, Leaderboard, User/Index, Tag/Index |
| Problem/Index row | Problem/Details/{id} |
| Problem/Details | Tag/Details (per tag chip), User/Details (via submission rows) |
| Leaderboard row | User/Details/{id} |
| User/Details | Submission/Details, Problem/Details |
| Submission/Index row | Submission/Details, User/Details, Problem/Details |
| Organization/Details | User/Details (members), ProblemSet/Details |
| ProblemSet/Details | Problem/Details, Organization/Details |
| Tag/Details | Problem/Details |

All inner pages include breadcrumbs via a `@section` or inline breadcrumb bar.

---

## Design implementation notes

- **Tailwind config** in `_Layout.cshtml` must include the exact color token map from the Stitch HTML files (already extracted above)
- **`border-radius: 0px` override**: Set `DEFAULT`, `lg`, `xl` all to `0px` in Tailwind config; `full` stays `9999px` for pill shapes only
- **No Tailwind purge in CDN mode** — all utility classes available without build step, appropriate for Razor views
- The solve_workspace editor code area uses an inline dark `bg-[#0e0e0e]` region — this is the only dark element on an otherwise light page

---

## Verification

1. `dotnet build WebIde.Web` → no errors
2. `dotnet run --project WebIde.Web` → app starts on `localhost:5xxx`
3. Navigate all routes: `/`, `/Problem`, `/Problem/Details/1–3`, `/Leaderboard`, `/User`, `/User/Details/1–3`, `/Organization`, `/Organization/Details/1–2`, `/ProblemSet`, `/ProblemSet/Details/1–2`, `/Submission`, `/Submission/Details/1–8`, `/Tag`, `/Tag/Details/1–4`
4. Visual check: all pages match Competitive Brutalism (hard shadows, zero radius, Space Grotesk headlines, no soft shadows/gradients)
5. The 4 Stitch-designed pages (Home, Problem/Index, Problem/Details, Leaderboard) closely match their `code.html` counterparts
6. `.claude/agents/ux-designer.md` exists and is committed
7. `lab-2/ux-agent-invocation.log` shows sub-agent was spawned
8. All nav links and cross-entity links resolve (no 404s)

--- name: ux-designer description: > Specialist sub-agent for generating and refining UI/UX code for the WebIde platform. Invoke this agent whenever generating or editing Razor views, CSS, or HTML layouts. It enforces the "Competitive Brutalism" design system defined in stitch_sharp_logic_arena/monoraw_hyper/DESIGN.md and produces production-quality, non-generic frontend code using the frontend-design skill. tools: Read, Edit, Write, Glob, Grep --- You are a specialized UI/UX code agent for the **WebIde** competitive coding platform. Your output must always follow the **Competitive Brutalism** design system ("Monoraw Hyper"). Before generating any UI, invoke the `frontend-design` skill as your base, then apply the overrides below. --- ## Design System: Competitive Brutalism ### North Star "The Raw Compiler" — raw, honest, high-velocity. Terminal + blueprint + brutalist architecture. No corporate softness. Information density is a feature. ### Color Tokens (Tailwind custom config) ```js colors: { "primary": "#2752c7", // Electric Blue — action, logic "on-primary": "#f1f2ff", "primary-container": "#7f9bff", "on-primary-container": "#001c5f", "secondary": "#b60055", // Hot Pink — critical highlights, CTA "on-secondary": "#ffeff1", "secondary-container": "#ffc1ce", "on-secondary-container": "#900042", "tertiary": "#106b00", // Lime Green — success, accepted "on-tertiary": "#d3ffc0", "tertiary-container": "#32ff00", "on-tertiary-container": "#0c5c00", "background": "#f6f6f6", "surface": "#f6f6f6", "surface-bright": "#f6f6f6", "surface-container-lowest": "#ffffff", "surface-container-low": "#f1f1f1", "surface-container": "#e8e8e8", "surface-container-high": "#e2e2e2", "surface-container-highest":"#dddddd", "surface-variant": "#dddddd", "on-surface": "#2f2f2f", "on-background": "#2f2f2f", "on-surface-variant": "#5b5b5b", "outline": "#777777", "outline-variant": "#adadad", "error": "#b41340", "error-container": "#f74b6d", "on-error": "#ffefef", "inverse-surface": "#0e0e0e", } ``` ### Typography - **Headlines / Labels / Nav**: `font-family: 'Space Grotesk', sans-serif` — bold, wide, engineered - **Body / Code**: `font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif` — quiet, legible - Labels and nav items must be **ALL-CAPS** (`uppercase` Tailwind class) - Display numbers (rankings, stats): `text-5xl font-black` minimum ### Borders & Elevation - **Prohibit 1px borders.** All structural borders: `border-2 border-black` (2px) or `border-4 border-black` (4px) - **Zero border-radius everywhere.** Tailwind config: `borderRadius: { DEFAULT: "0px", lg: "0px", xl: "0px" }` - Exception: `rounded-full` only for circular avatar/icon wrappers where explicitly needed - **Hard offset shadows only.** Use `shadow-[4px_4px_0px_0px_rgba(0,0,0,1)]` or `shadow-[8px_8px_0px_0px_rgba(0,0,0,1)]` - Hover lift: `hover:translate-x-[-2px] hover:translate-y-[-2px] hover:shadow-[6px_6px_0px_0px_rgba(0,0,0,1)]` - Active press: `active:translate-x-[2px] active:translate-y-[2px] active:shadow-none` - **No gradients. No glassmorphism. No soft shadows.** Flat solid fills only. ### Components #### Buttons ```html ``` #### Difficulty Badge Pills ```html EASY MEDIUM HARD ``` #### Submission Status Chips ```html ACCEPTED WRONG_ANSWER TLE ``` #### Cards ```html
``` #### Tables ```html
COLUMN
...
``` #### Code Block (Dark Editor Area) ```html
``` ### Navigation Structure - **Top nav**: fixed `h-20`, `bg-[#f6f6f6]`, `border-b-4 border-black`, `shadow-[4px_4px_0px_0px_rgba(0,0,0,1)]` - **Left sidebar** (inner pages only): fixed `w-64`, `border-r-4 border-black`, `pt-24` - Active nav item: `bg-primary text-white border-2 border-black` or `border-b-4 border-primary text-primary` - **Footer**: `bg-black text-white border-t-4 border-black`, Space Grotesk uppercase links ### Spacing - Use large gaps: `gap-6`, `gap-8` between bordered containers - Section padding: `p-6` or `p-8` inside cards; `px-6` for nav items --- ## Razor/MVC Rules - Use `asp-controller` and `asp-action` Tag Helpers for all internal links — never hardcode URLs - Use `@Model.Property` for data binding; keep logic minimal in views (only `if`/`foreach`) - `@await Html.PartialAsync("_Sidebar")` to inject sidebar on inner pages - All pages must set `ViewData["Title"]` for the `` tag --- ## DO NOT - Use rounded corners (no `rounded-md`, `rounded-lg`, etc.) - Use soft box-shadows (`shadow-md`, `shadow-lg`, etc.) - Use gradients or backdrop-filter - Use Bootstrap — only Tailwind CDN - Use 1px borders - Mix dark/light modes — this is a **light mode** design (background `#f6f6f6`)

@model List @{ ViewData["Title"] = "PROBLEMS"; // Acceptance rate per problem: Accepted / Total submissions double Rate(WebIde.Model.Problem p) => p.Submissions.Count == 0 ? 0 : Math.Round((double)p.Submissions.Count(s => s.Status == WebIde.Model.Enums.SubmissionStatus.Accepted) / p.Submissions.Count * 100, 1); } @await Html.PartialAsync("_Sidebar")

PROBLEMS_LIBRARY

HOME / PROBLEMS
@{ int total = Model.Count; int accepted = Model.Sum(p => p.Submissions.Count(s => s.Status == WebIde.Model.Enums.SubmissionStatus.Accepted)); int totalSubs = Model.Sum(p => p.Submissions.Count); double overallRate = totalSubs == 0 ? 0 : Math.Round((double)accepted / totalSubs * 100, 1); }

TOTAL_PROBLEMS

@total

ACCEPTANCE_RATE

@overallRate%

TOTAL_SUBMISSIONS

@totalSubs

filter_list FILTER_CONTROLS

@foreach (var problem in Model) { var hasAccepted = problem.Submissions.Any(s => s.Status == WebIde.Model.Enums.SubmissionStatus.Accepted); var rate = Rate(problem); }
STATUS PROBLEM_NAME DIFFICULTY ACCEPTANCE ACTION
@if (hasAccepted) { check_circle } else if (problem.Submissions.Any()) { cancel } else { radio_button_unchecked }
@($"{problem.Id:000}_{problem.Title.ToUpper().Replace(" ", "_")}")
@foreach (var tag in problem.Tags) { #@tag.Name.ToUpper().Replace(" ", "_") }
@switch (problem.Difficulty) { case DifficultyLevel.Easy: EASY break; case DifficultyLevel.Medium: MEDIUM break; case DifficultyLevel.Hard: HARD break; } @rate% SOLVE
SHOWING_@(Model.Count)_ENTRIES

@model WebIde.Model.Problem @{ ViewData["Title"] = Model.Title.ToUpper(); var sampleCases = Model.TestCases.Where(tc => tc.IsSample).OrderBy(tc => tc.OrderIndex).ToList(); // Most recent submission for the console tab var lastSub = Model.Submissions.OrderByDescending(s => s.SubmittedAt).FirstOrDefault(); } @await Html.PartialAsync("_Sidebar")
HOME / PROBLEMS / @Model.Title.ToUpper().Replace(" ", "_")
@switch (Model.Difficulty) { case DifficultyLevel.Easy: EASY break; case DifficultyLevel.Medium: MEDIUM break; case DifficultyLevel.Hard: HARD break; } TASK_ID: @Model.Id

@Model.Title.ToUpper().Replace(" ", "_")

schedule @(Model.TimeLimitMs / 1000.0)s
memory @(Model.MemoryLimitKb / 1024)MB
person @Model.AuthorUsername

DESCRIPTION

@Model.Description

@if (sampleCases.Any()) {

SAMPLE_TEST_CASES

@foreach (var tc in sampleCases) {

EXAMPLE_@tc.OrderIndex

INPUT: @tc.InputArgs

OUTPUT: @tc.ExpectedOutput

POINTS: @tc.Points

}
}

TAGS

@foreach (var tag in Model.Tags) { @tag.Name }

TOP_SOLVERS:

@foreach (var sub in Model.Submissions.Where(s => s.Status == WebIde.Model.Enums.SubmissionStatus.Accepted).OrderBy(s => s.WallTimeMs).Take(3)) {
@sub.User.DisplayName @sub.WallTimeMs ms
}
solution.cpp
C++_17
@if (lastSub != null) {
@for (int i = 1; i <= lastSub.SourceCode.Split('\n').Length; i++) { @i
} 
@lastSub.SourceCode
} else {
// NO_SUBMISSIONS_YET
}
CONSOLE TEST_CASES (@Model.TestCases.Count) SUBMISSIONS (@Model.Submissions.Count)
@if (lastSub != null) {

>>> LAST_SUBMISSION — @lastSub.Status.ToString().ToUpper()

@if (lastSub.ExecutionResult != null) {

STDOUT: @(string.IsNullOrEmpty(lastSub.ExecutionResult.Stdout) ? "(empty)" : lastSub.ExecutionResult.Stdout)

EXIT: @lastSub.ExecutionResult.ExitCode

TIME: @lastSub.WallTimeMs ms

MEM: @lastSub.PeakMemoryKb KB

} } else {

>>> NO_SUBMISSIONS_YET. Submit a solution to see results here.

}

ALL_SUBMISSIONS:

@foreach (var sub in Model.Submissions.OrderByDescending(s => s.SubmittedAt)) {
@sub.User.Username @switch (sub.Status) { case WebIde.Model.Enums.SubmissionStatus.Accepted: ACCEPTED break; case WebIde.Model.Enums.SubmissionStatus.WrongAnswer: WRONG_ANSWER break; case WebIde.Model.Enums.SubmissionStatus.TimeLimitExceeded: TLE break; case WebIde.Model.Enums.SubmissionStatus.CompileError: COMPILE_ERROR break; default: @sub.Status.ToString().ToUpper() break; } @sub.WallTimeMs ms
}

LEADERBOARD

Global_Rankings_v.2.0

HOME / LEADERBOARD
@if (top3.Any()) {
@{ // Render rank 2 first (left), rank 1 center, rank 3 right — match Stitch design var ordered = new[] { top3.ElementAtOrDefault(1), top3.ElementAtOrDefault(0), top3.ElementAtOrDefault(2) } .Where(e => e != null).ToList(); } @foreach (var entry in ordered) { bool isFirst = entry!.Rank == 1;
@entry.Rank.ToString("00") @PodiumIcon(entry.Rank)
@entry.User.Username.ToUpper()
