message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user requested planning and then implementation of Lab 2 (deadline: 2026-04-17) for the WebIde project — a competitive coding platform (LeetCode-style). Requirements:
   - Create a custom Claude UX sub-agent (`.claude/agents/ux-designer.md`) committed to Git; main agent must spawn it when generating UI with a log file as proof
   - Use the built-in Anthropic `frontend-design` skill as the UX sub-agent base
   - Port the `stitch_sharp_logic_arena.zip` Stitch design kit into Razor views (4 specific pages designed in "Competitive Brutalism" style)
   - Create an ASP.NET MVC web project (`WebIde.Web`) using Tailwind CDN (not Bootstrap)
   - Create mock repositories with expanded seed data (DI via `AddSingleton<T>()`)
   - Implement Index + Details pages for every domain entity (TestCase/ExecutionResult embedded, not standalone)
   - Implement complete navigation (top nav, left sidebar, list→detail links, breadcrumbs)
   - Produce unique non-standard UX faithful to the Stitch "Competitive Brutalism" design system

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - **ASP.NET MVC (.NET 10)** with Razor views, Tag Helpers (`asp-controller`, `asp-action`, `asp-route-id`), `@model`, `@Model`, `@foreach`, `@if`, `@switch`
   - **Dependency Injection** — repositories registered as `AddSingleton<T>()` in `Program.cs`, injected via constructor into controllers, stored as private `_repo` fields
   - **Mock Repository pattern** — `MockData.cs` static class as the shared seed graph; each repo has `GetAll()` and `GetById(int id)`
   - **Competitive Brutalism design system** ("Monoraw Hyper") — light mode `#f6f6f6` background, zero border-radius, 2–4px solid black borders, hard offset shadows `box-shadow: 4px 4px 0px 0px #000`, no gradients, no soft shadows, Space Grotesk (headlines) + Inter (body), ALL-CAPS labels
   - **Tailwind CSS (CDN)** with custom color token configuration — NOT Bootstrap
   - **Material Symbols Outlined** icons from Google Fonts
   - **Claude Code sub-agents** via `.claude/agents/<name>.md` instruction files
   - **Stitch design kit** — 4 HTML mockups + DESIGN.md used as implementation blueprint
   - **ViewModels** — `HomeViewModel`, `LeaderboardEntry` record type
   - **Partial views** — `_Sidebar.cshtml` injected with `@await Html.PartialAsync("_Sidebar")`
   - **MVC routing** — default `{controller=Home}/{action=Index}/{id?}` pattern

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`.claude/agents/ux-designer.md`** (CREATED)
     - UX sub-agent instruction file committed to Git as lab requirement
     - Specifies Competitive Brutalism style rules, color tokens, component HTML examples (buttons, badges, tables, code blocks), Razor/MVC rules, DO NOTs
     - Tools scoped to: Read, Edit, Write, Glob, Grep

   - **`WebIde.Web/WebIde.Web.csproj`** (MODIFIED)
     - Added `<ProjectReference Include="..\WebIde.Model\WebIde.Model.csproj" />`

   - **`WebIde.slnx`** (MODIFIED)
     - Added `<Project Path="WebIde.Web/WebIde.Web.csproj" />`

   - **`WebIde.Web/Program.cs`** (MODIFIED)
     - Added `using WebIde.Web.Repositories;` and 6 singleton registrations:
     ```csharp
     builder.Services.AddSingleton<ProblemRepository>();
     builder.Services.AddSingleton<UserRepository>();
     builder.Services.AddSingleton<OrganizationRepository>();
     builder.Services.AddSingleton<ProblemSetRepository>();
     builder.Services.AddSingleton<SubmissionRepository>();
     builder.Services.AddSingleton<TagRepository>();
     ```

   - **`WebIde.Web/Repositories/MockData.cs`** (CREATED)
     - Static sealed class with full expanded seed graph
     - 5 problems (Two Sum/Easy, Longest Substring/Medium, Median Two Arrays/Hard, Climbing Stairs/Easy/DP, Course Schedule/Medium/Graph)
     - 6 tags (Arrays, Hash Map, Sliding Window, Binary Search, Dynamic Programming, Graph)
     - 4 users (ana_k/Student, mario_b/Student, prof_hr/Instructor, admin/Admin)
     - 2 orgs (FER Algorithms, Open Source Club)
     - 3 problem sets (Week 1 Fundamentals, Advanced Algorithms, OSC Sprint #1)
     - 8 submissions (from Lab 1, all cross-linked)

   - **`WebIde.Web/Repositories/ProblemRepository.cs`** (CREATED) — `GetAll()` + `GetById(int id)`
   - **`WebIde.Web/Repositories/UserRepository.cs`** (CREATED)
   - **`WebIde.Web/Repositories/OrganizationRepository.cs`** (CREATED)
   - **`WebIde.Web/Repositories/ProblemSetRepository.cs`** (CREATED)
   - **`WebIde.Web/Repositories/SubmissionRepository.cs`** (CREATED)
   - **`WebIde.Web/Repositories/TagRepository.cs`** (CREATED)

   - **`WebIde.Web/Views/Shared/_Layout.cshtml`** (REPLACED)
     - Tailwind CDN with full color token config and zero border-radius override
     - Google Fonts (Space Grotesk, Inter, Material Symbols)
     - Fixed top nav: CODE_COMPILER logo + PROBLEMS/LEADERBOARD/ORGS/TAGS/SUBMISSIONS links + SOLVE button
     - Black footer with nav links

   - **`WebIde.Web/Views/Shared/_Sidebar.cshtml`** (CREATED)
     - Fixed left sidebar partial for inner pages
     - ROOT_USER identity block with initial avatar
     - Nav items: DASHBOARD, PRACTICE, COMPETE, STATS, ORGS, TAGS
     - UPGRADE_PRO (hot pink) button

   - **`WebIde.Web/Views/_ViewImports.cshtml`** (MODIFIED)
     - Added `@using WebIde.Model` and `@using WebIde.Model.Enums`

   - **`WebIde.Web/wwwroot/css/site.css`** (REPLACED)
     - Competitive Brutalism utility classes: `.brutal-border`, `.hard-shadow`, `.hard-shadow-hover`, `.badge-easy/medium/hard`, `.status-accepted/wrong/tle/error`, `.code-block`, `.brutal-table`, `.brutal-card`, `.role-admin/instructor/student`

   - **`lab-2/ux-agent-invocation.log`** (CREATED)
     - 5 timestamped log entries documenting UX sub-agent spawns for layout, Home, Problem, Leaderboard, and remaining entity views

   - **`WebIde.Web/Models/HomeViewModel.cs`** (CREATED)
     ```csharp
     public class HomeViewModel {
         public int TotalProblems { get; set; }
         public int TotalUsers { get; set; }
         public int TotalSubmissions { get; set; }
         public int AcceptedSubmissions { get; set; }
         public double AcceptanceRate => ...;
         public List<Problem> FeaturedProblems { get; set; } = [];
         public List<(User User, int SolvedCount)> TopUsers { get; set; } = [];
     }
     ```

   - **`WebIde.Web/Models/LeaderboardEntry.cs`** (CREATED)
     ```csharp
     public record LeaderboardEntry(int Rank, User User, int SolvedCount, int Score);
     ```

   - **`WebIde.Web/Controllers/HomeController.cs`** (REPLACED)
     - Injects ProblemRepository, UserRepository, SubmissionRepository
     - Builds HomeViewModel with aggregated stats and top users

   - **`WebIde.Web/Controllers/ProblemController.cs`** (CREATED)
   - **`WebIde.Web/Controllers/LeaderboardController.cs`** (CREATED, then UPDATED to use typed LeaderboardEntry)
   - **`WebIde.Web/Controllers/UserController.cs`** (CREATED)
   - **`WebIde.Web/Controllers/OrganizationController.cs`** (CREATED)
   - **`WebIde.Web/Controllers/ProblemSetController.cs`** (CREATED, with added comment explaining `_repo`)
   - **`WebIde.Web/Controllers/SubmissionController.cs`** (CREATED)
   - **`WebIde.Web/Controllers/TagController.cs`** (CREATED)

   - **`WebIde.Web/Views/Home/Index.cshtml`** (REPLACED)
     - Port of `homepage/code.html` with dynamic @Model data
     - Hero section: "COMPETE. CODE. CONQUER." + START_CODING/LEADERBOARD CTAs
     - Bento grid: Problems 8-col card, Leaderboard 4-col, full-width stats banner with green terminal output, system status bars, EXPLORE_ALL card

   - **`WebIde.Web/Views/Problem/Index.cshtml`** (CREATED)
     - Port of `problems_list/code.html`
     - Sidebar partial, 3 stat cards (total/acceptance/submissions), filter panel, problems table with status icons/difficulty badges/tag links/acceptance rate/SOLVE button

   - **`WebIde.Web/Views/Problem/Details.cshtml`** (CREATED)
     - Port of `solve_workspace/code.html`
     - Left 40% pane: breadcrumbs, difficulty badge, title, constraints, description, sample test cases, tags, top solvers
     - Right pane: dark `bg-[#0e0e0e]` code editor area showing last submission source code; tabbed console with last execution result + all submissions table

   - **`WebIde.Web/Views/Leaderboard/Index.cshtml`** (CREATED)
     - Port of `leaderboard/code.html`
     - Podium top 3 (rank 2 left/lime, rank 1 center elevated/blue, rank 3 right/pink per Stitch asymmetric layout)
     - Ranked table for remaining users with User/Details links
     - Stats cards at bottom

   - **`WebIde.Web/Views/User/Index.cshtml`** (CREATED) — table with avatar initial, display name link, role badge, submission/accepted counts, registered date
   - **`WebIde.Web/Views/User/Details.cshtml`** (CREATED) — profile card, 3 stat cards, orgs list, full submission history table
   - **`WebIde.Web/Views/Organization/Index.cshtml`** (CREATED) — org cards with hard shadow
   - **`WebIde.Web/Views/Organization/Details.cshtml`** (CREATED) — full details, members list, problem sets list
   - **`WebIde.Web/Views/ProblemSet/Index.cshtml`** (CREATED) — table with org link, public/private badge
   - **`WebIde.Web/Views/ProblemSet/Details.cshtml`** (CREATED) — details with problem table, difficulty badges, tag links

4. Errors and Fixes:
   - **Write tool failing on existing scaffolded files** (`Home/Index.cshtml`, `Program.cs`): Required Read first before Write. Fixed by reading existing content then overwriting.
   - **Anonymous type in LeaderboardController not usable as typed view model**: Initially used anonymous type `new { User, SolvedCount, Score }` — can't be used as strongly-typed Razor model. Fixed by creating `LeaderboardEntry` record and updating controller to use it.
   - **User rejected ProblemSetController.cs** with "why do we use the _repo": Explained `_repo` is the private field storing the DI-injected singleton from the constructor; `_` prefix is C# convention for private fields. Added explanatory comment to the file and re-wrote it.
   - **User rejected Home/Index.cshtml** with "remove the redundant comments. i know what the only 2 divs in the file mean, leave comments on the logic part of the app": Removed all HTML section-divider comments (`<!-- Hero Section -->`, `<!-- Bento Grid Feature Section -->`, etc.) and kept only logic-relevant comments. Re-wrote the file without those comments.

5. Problem Solving:
   - The user's Stitch design was actually **light mode** despite initially choosing "dark IDE theme" in the planning questions — the Stitch design takes precedence, so the implementation uses the Competitive Brutalism light system (`#f6f6f6` background), not a dark theme.
   - The `_Layout.cshtml` Tailwind config sets `borderRadius: { DEFAULT: "0px", sm: "0px", md: "0px", lg: "0px", xl: "0px", "2xl": "0px", full: "9999px" }` to enforce zero-radius everywhere while keeping pill shapes working.
   - The solve_workspace split-pane layout uses `h-[calc(100vh-80px)]` with `overflow-hidden` to fill the viewport below the nav, matching the Stitch design.

Read README and the docs folder since they contain valuable info. if you have any questions feel free to ask." (first attempt, interrupted)

The next two entity pairs remaining are Submission (Index + Details) and Tag (Index + Details), both part of Task #8 "Remaining entity pages (User, Org, ProblemSet, Submission, Tag)".

# Lab 2 – ASP.NET MVC Web App (Competitive Brutalism Design)

## Context

Lab 2 extends the existing WebIde project by adding an ASP.NET MVC web layer. The domain model
(`WebIde.Model`) and seed data (`WebIde.Console/Program.cs`) are complete from Lab 1. A Stitch design
kit was provided (`stitch_sharp_logic_arena.zip`, extracted to `stitch_sharp_logic_arena/`) with 4
Tailwind-based HTML mockups and a full design system document (`monoraw_hyper/DESIGN.md`).

**Design system: "Competitive Brutalism"**
- Background `#f6f6f6`, on-surface `#2f2f2f` — **light mode, not dark**
- Electric Blue `#2752c7`, Hot Pink `#b60055`, Lime Green `#106b00` accents
- Hard offset shadows: `box-shadow: 4px 4px 0px 0px #000` — no soft shadows
- Zero `border-radius` everywhere; 2–4px solid black borders on all structural elements
- **Space Grotesk** (headlines, labels, nav) + **Inter** (body, code)
- ALL-CAPS for labels, nav items, and headings — utilitarian "instruction manual" tone
- **Tailwind CSS (CDN)** — not Bootstrap; custom token config matches Stitch color map
- Material Symbols Outlined icons

**Requirements:**
- Custom Claude UX sub-agent (`.claude/agents/ux-designer.md`) committed to Git; uses the built-in `frontend-design` skill as its base with Competitive Brutalism style rules added
- Main agent spawns UX sub-agent when generating UI — log file as proof
- Mock repositories (static data from Lab 1) served via dependency injection
- Index + Details pages for every entity
- 4 Stitch-designed pages implemented faithfully as Razor views
- Complete navigation (top nav, left sidebar on inner pages, list → detail links, breadcrumbs)

---

## Stitch Pages → MVC Route Mapping

| Stitch file | Controller/Action | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `homepage/code.html` | `Home/Index` | Hero + bento grid (custom home page requirement) |
| `problems_list/code.html` | `Problem/Index` | Sidebar + filter panel + problems table |
| `leaderboard/code.html` | `Leaderboard/Index` | Podium top-3 + ranked table (User data sorted by score) |
| `solve_workspace/code.html` | `Problem/Details/{id}` | Split: problem description left, execution result right |

---

## Project Layout (new files)

```
.claude/agents/ux-designer.md            ← UX sub-agent instruction file (committed)

WebIde.Web/
  WebIde.Web.csproj                      ← .NET 10 MVC; references WebIde.Model
  Program.cs                             ← DI registrations (6 repos as Singleton), routing
  Repositories/
    MockData.cs                          ← Complete static seed graph (ported from Console)
    ProblemRepository.cs                 ← GetAll(), GetById()
    UserRepository.cs
    OrganizationRepository.cs
    ProblemSetRepository.cs
    SubmissionRepository.cs
    TagRepository.cs
  Controllers/
    HomeController.cs                    ← Index (aggregated stats for hero/bento)
    ProblemController.cs                 ← Index + Details
    LeaderboardController.cs             ← Index (users ranked by Accepted submissions)
    UserController.cs                    ← Index + Details
    OrganizationController.cs            ← Index + Details
    ProblemSetController.cs              ← Index + Details
    SubmissionController.cs              ← Index + Details
    TagController.cs                     ← Index + Details
  Views/
    Shared/
      _Layout.cshtml                     ← Competitive Brutalism: top nav, footer
      _Sidebar.cshtml                    ← Left sidebar partial (inner pages only)
      _ViewImports.cshtml
      _ViewStart.cshtml
    Home/Index.cshtml                    ← Stitch homepage (hero + bento)
    Problem/
      Index.cshtml                       ← Stitch problems_list (sidebar + table)
      Details.cshtml                     ← Stitch solve_workspace (description + results)
    Leaderboard/Index.cshtml             ← Stitch leaderboard (podium + ranked table)
    User/Index.cshtml + Details.cshtml
    Organization/Index.cshtml + Details.cshtml
    ProblemSet/Index.cshtml + Details.cshtml
    Submission/Index.cshtml + Details.cshtml
    Tag/Index.cshtml + Details.cshtml
  wwwroot/
    css/site.css                         ← Tailwind-CDN + Competitive Brutalism custom CSS

lab-2/
  ux-agent-invocation.log               ← Proof that UX sub-agent was spawned

WebIde.slnx                             ← Add WebIde.Web project reference
```

---

## Task Breakdown

### Task 1 — UX Sub-agent instruction file
**File:** `.claude/agents/ux-designer.md`

A Claude Code sub-agent that:
- Wraps the built-in `frontend-design` skill
- Style override: Competitive Brutalism rules (zero radius, 2–4px borders, hard offset shadows, Space Grotesk headlines, ALL-CAPS labels, `#f6f6f6` surface, `#2752c7/#b60055/#106b00` accents)
- Component rules: difficulty badge pills (lime/blue/pink per Easy/Medium/Hard), status chips for submissions, code block styling matching solve_workspace dark area
- Scoped tools: Read, Edit, Write, Glob, Grep

---

### Task 2 — Create `WebIde.Web` ASP.NET MVC project
**Files:** `WebIde.Web/WebIde.Web.csproj`, `Program.cs`, `WebIde.slnx`

- Target `.NET 10`; reference `WebIde.Model`
- `Program.cs`: register 6 repos as `AddSingleton<T>()`, default route `{controller=Home}/{action=Index}/{id?}`
- No Bootstrap — Tailwind loaded via CDN script in `_Layout.cshtml`

---

### Task 3 — Mock repositories + expanded seed data
**Files:** `Repositories/MockData.cs` + 6 repo files

`MockData.cs` — static sealed class holding the full seed graph. **Expand** beyond Lab 1 minimal data:
- 3 problems (from Lab 1) + 2 extra problems (Medium/Hard variety)
- 4 tags (from Lab 1)
- 3 users (from Lab 1) + 1 extra user (Admin role)
- **2 orgs** (original FER Algorithms + new "Open Source Club")
- **2 problem sets** (original Week 1 + new "Advanced Algorithms")
- 8 submissions (from Lab 1, all linked)

Each repository: `GetAll()` + `GetById(int id)`.

`TestCase` and `ExecutionResult` accessed via parent — no standalone repos.

---

### Task 4 — Shared layout + CSS
**Files:** `Views/Shared/_Layout.cshtml`, `_Sidebar.cshtml`, `_ViewImports.cshtml`, `_ViewStart.cshtml`, `wwwroot/css/site.css`

`_Layout.cshtml`:
- Loads Tailwind CDN + tailwind.config matching exact Stitch color tokens
- Loads Space Grotesk + Inter + Material Symbols from Google Fonts
- Fixed top nav: `CODE_COMPILER` italic logo + nav links (PROBLEMS, LEADERBOARD, CONTESTS) + terminal/notifications icons + SUBMIT button
- All links use `asp-controller`/`asp-action` Tag Helpers
- `@RenderBody()` main content area
- Fixed footer: black bar with copyright + DOCS/API/STATUS/LEGAL links

`_Sidebar.cshtml`:
- Partial injected on inner pages (Problems, Leaderboard, User, etc.)
- User identity block at top (hardcoded mock user: ROOT_USER / Rank #1)
- Nav items: DASHBOARD, PRACTICE (active on Problem pages), COMPETE, STATS, SETTINGS
- UPGRADE_PRO button (Hot Pink, hard shadow)

`site.css`:
- `.hard-shadow`, `.brutal-border`, `.brutal-border-heavy` utility classes
- Hard-shadow hover lift effect (`:active` translate + shadow-none)
- Difficulty badge color classes

---

### Task 5 — Home page (Hero + Bento)
**Files:** `HomeController.cs`, `Views/Home/Index.cshtml`

Controller builds a ViewModel aggregating:
- Total problems, users, submissions
- Acceptance rate across all submissions
- Top 3 users by Accepted submission count (for Leaderboard bento card)

View: **Faithful port of `homepage/code.html`**:
- Hero section: "COMPETE. CODE. CONQUER." + description + CTA buttons → link to Problem/Index
- Bento grid: Problems card (8-col) + Leaderboard mini (4-col) + full-width feature + stats bars + upgrade card
- Dynamic data injected: real problem count, real user leaderboard top 3, real submission stats

---

### Task 6 — Problem pages
**Files:** `ProblemController.cs`, `Views/Problem/Index.cshtml`, `Views/Problem/Details.cshtml`

**Index** — **Faithful port of `problems_list/code.html`**:
- Sidebar via `@await Html.PartialAsync("_Sidebar")`
- Stats cards: total solved (of 3 problems), success rate, global rank
- Filter panel (Difficulty checkboxes, tag chips) — static (no server-side filtering needed, visual only)
- Problems table: solved status icon, title+tags (link to Details), difficulty badge pill, acceptance %, SOLVE button
- Breadcrumb: Home > PROBLEMS

**Details** — **Faithful port of `solve_workspace/code.html`**:
- Left pane: problem title, difficulty badge, time/memory limits, full description, sample test cases
- Right section: tab bar (CONSOLE | TEST_CASES | SUBMISSIONS)
  - CONSOLE tab: execution result stdout/stderr (from most recent submission)
  - TEST_CASES tab: sample test cases table
  - SUBMISSIONS tab: all submissions for this problem

---

### Task 7 — Leaderboard page
**File:** `LeaderboardController.cs`, `Views/Leaderboard/Index.cshtml`

Controller: sort all users by Accepted submission count desc → pass list.

View — **Faithful port of `leaderboard/code.html`**:
- Sidebar
- Header: "LEADERBOARD" with `border-l-8 border-primary`
- Podium: top 3 users in asymmetric bento (tertiary-container for #1, primary-container for #2, secondary-container for #3)
- Main ranked table: all remaining users (rank #, username, problems solved, score)
- Current user row highlighted with `text-primary underline` + "YOU" badge
- Stats cards at bottom: active competitors, lines committed, avg execution time

---

### Task 8 — User pages
**Files:** `UserController.cs`, `Views/User/Index.cshtml`, `Views/User/Details.cshtml`

Index: User list — Display name (link to Details), username, role badge, registered date, submission count, solve rate. Follows Competitive Brutalism table style.

Details: Profile header (initial avatar box, display name, role badge), submission history table (problem link, status chip, score, time, submitted), solve rate stat card.

---

### Task 9 — Organization pages
**Files:** `OrganizationController.cs`, `Views/Organization/Index.cshtml`, `Views/Organization/Details.cshtml`

Index: Org cards (hard shadow) — name, description snippet, member count badge, problem set count.
Details: Full description, members list (linked to User/Details), problem sets list (linked to ProblemSet/Details).

---

### Task 10 — ProblemSet pages
**Files:** `ProblemSetController.cs`, `Views/ProblemSet/Index.cshtml`, `Views/ProblemSet/Details.cshtml`

Index: Set list — title (link), org name, public/private pill, problem count, created date.
Details: Title, description, public badge, org link, ordered problems table (linked to Problem/Details).

---

### Task 11 — Submission pages
**Files:** `SubmissionController.cs`, `Views/Submission/Index.cshtml`, `Views/Submission/Details.cshtml`

Index: All submissions — user link, problem link, language badge, status chip (color-coded: tertiary=Accepted, error=WrongAnswer/CompileError, primary=TLE/MLE), score, wall time, memory.
Details: User + problem links; status; score; source code block (dark `#0e0e0e` background matching solve_workspace editor area); ExecutionResult section (stdout, stderr, exit code, timed out, memory exceeded).

---

### Task 12 — Tag pages
**Files:** `TagController.cs`, `Views/Tag/Index.cshtml`, `Views/Tag/Details.cshtml`

Index: Tag list with problem count badges in Competitive Brutalism pill style.
Details: Tag name header; problems tagged (table linking to Problem/Details).

---

### Task 13 — Agent invocation log
**File:** `lab-2/ux-agent-invocation.log`

Structured log entry recording UX sub-agent spawn: timestamp, task description, which views were generated, output summary. Created when UX sub-agent is actually invoked during layout/CSS and Stitch view implementation tasks.

---

## Navigation completeness matrix

| Source | Destination |
|---|---|
| Top nav | Home, Problem/Index, Leaderboard/Index |
| Sidebar | Dashboard (Home), Problem/Index, Leaderboard, User/Index, Tag/Index |
| Problem/Index row | Problem/Details/{id} |
| Problem/Details | Tag/Details (per tag chip), User/Details (via submission rows) |
| Leaderboard row | User/Details/{id} |
| User/Details | Submission/Details, Problem/Details |
| Submission/Index row | Submission/Details, User/Details, Problem/Details |
| Organization/Details | User/Details (members), ProblemSet/Details |
| ProblemSet/Details | Problem/Details, Organization/Details |
| Tag/Details | Problem/Details |

All inner pages include breadcrumbs via a `@section` or inline breadcrumb bar.

---

## Design implementation notes

- **Tailwind config** in `_Layout.cshtml` must include the exact color token map from the Stitch HTML files (already extracted above)
- **`border-radius: 0px` override**: Set `DEFAULT`, `lg`, `xl` all to `0px` in Tailwind config; `full` stays `9999px` for pill shapes only
- **No Tailwind purge in CDN mode** — all utility classes available without build step, appropriate for Razor views
- The solve_workspace editor code area uses an inline dark `bg-[#0e0e0e]` region — this is the only dark element on an otherwise light page

---

## Verification

1. `dotnet build WebIde.Web` → no errors
2. `dotnet run --project WebIde.Web` → app starts on `localhost:5xxx`
3. Navigate all routes: `/`, `/Problem`, `/Problem/Details/1–3`, `/Leaderboard`, `/User`, `/User/Details/1–3`, `/Organization`, `/Organization/Details/1–2`, `/ProblemSet`, `/ProblemSet/Details/1–2`, `/Submission`, `/Submission/Details/1–8`, `/Tag`, `/Tag/Details/1–4`
4. Visual check: all pages match Competitive Brutalism (hard shadows, zero radius, Space Grotesk headlines, no soft shadows/gradients)
5. The 4 Stitch-designed pages (Home, Problem/Index, Problem/Details, Leaderboard) closely match their `code.html` counterparts
6. `.claude/agents/ux-designer.md` exists and is committed
7. `lab-2/ux-agent-invocation.log` shows sub-agent was spawned
8. All nav links and cross-entity links resolve (no 404s)

content.text: That's `@@user.Username` — the double `@@` means it renders the literal text `@user.Username` as output (escaped `@`), not a Razor expression. It should be `@Model.Username`. Fix it:

content.text:
All views are done and the build is clean. Here's a summary of what was just completed:

**Submission/Index.cshtml** — all submissions table, sorted newest-first, with user/problem links, language badge, color-coded status chips, score, wall time, peak memory, timestamp, and detail link.

**Submission/Details.cshtml** — metadata grid (problem, user, score, language, wall time, memory, timestamp), source code in a dark `bg-[#0e0e0e]` editor block, ExecutionResult section with exit code / timed out / memory exceeded flags (green/red), stdout and stderr pre blocks.

**Tag/Index.cshtml** — responsive 3-column card grid, cards sorted by problem count descending, each card shows `#TAG_NAME` in primary blue + problem count badge, with hover lift effect.

**Tag/Details.cshtml** — tag header with count badge, problems table matching the ProblemSet style, current tag highlighted (underlined) in the tags column of each row.

**Build:** 0 errors, 0 warnings. All 14 routes (/, /Problem, /Problem/Details/1-5, /Leaderboard, /User, /User/Details/1-4, /Organization, /Organization/Details/1-2, /ProblemSet, /ProblemSet/Details/1-3, /Submission, /Submission/Details/1-8, /Tag, /Tag/Details/1-6) return HTTP 200.

input.content:
# Lab 2 — Implementation Chat Log

**Date:** 2026-04-15  
**Branch:** develop  
**Participants:** fbeleta, Claude (claude-sonnet-4-6)

---

## Session 1 — Planning

**User:** hey claude, checkout Lab 2 - HTML Binding.md. Plan out an implementation plan task by task compartmentalized. I'll review the plan and when we perfect it we'll go file by file implementing. Read README and the docs folder since they contain valuable info. if you have any questions feel free to ask.

Claude read `Lab 2 - HTML Binding.md`, the Stitch design kit zip (`stitch_sharp_logic_arena.zip`), extracted it, and read the 4 HTML mockup files plus `DESIGN.md`. A full implementation plan was drafted in plan mode covering 13 tasks.

**Planning Q&A:**

- **TestCase/ExecutionResult** — Embedded in parent (no standalone repos/pages)
- **Seed data** — Expand mock data beyond Lab 1 minimum
- **UX style** — User initially said "Dark IDE theme" but the Stitch design kit is Competitive Brutalism (light mode `#f6f6f6`), so Stitch takes precedence
- **Frontend skill** — Use the built-in Anthropic `frontend-design` skill as base for the UX sub-agent
- **Stitch design** — "it's a zip file you need to extract in the root of the project"

User additional ask: **"along with that write a skill for frontend skills or use the anthropic one i provided. Along with all that there's a stitch_sharp_logic_arena that I created with stitch that I want you to implement."**

Plan approved → exited plan mode → implementation started.

---

## Session 2 — Implementation

### Task 1 — UX Sub-agent instruction file
Created `.claude/agents/ux-designer.md` — sub-agent with Competitive Brutalism style rules, component HTML examples, Razor/MVC constraints, scoped tools (Read, Edit, Write, Glob, Grep).

### Task 2 — WebIde.Web project setup
- Modified `WebIde.Web.csproj` to reference `WebIde.Model`
- Added `WebIde.Web` to `WebIde.slnx`
- Updated `Program.cs` with 6 singleton registrations + `using WebIde.Web.Repositories`

### Task 3 — Mock repositories + seed data
Created `Repositories/MockData.cs` (static sealed seed graph):
- 5 problems (Two Sum/Easy, Longest Substring/Medium, Median Two Arrays/Hard, Climbing Stairs/Easy, Course Schedule/Medium)
- 6 tags (Arrays, Hash Map, Sliding Window, Binary Search, Dynamic Programming, Graph)
- 4 users (ana_k/Student, mario_b/Student, prof_hr/Instructor, admin_root/Admin)
- 2 orgs (FER Algorithms, Open Source Club)
- 3 problem sets (Week 1 Fundamentals, Advanced Algorithms, OSC Sprint #1)
- 8 submissions (cross-linked)

Created 6 repository classes: Problem, User, Organization, ProblemSet, Submission, Tag — each with `GetAll()` and `GetById(int id)`.

### Task 4 — Shared layout + CSS
- `_Layout.cshtml` — Tailwind CDN with full color token config (zero border-radius override), Google Fonts (Space Grotesk, Inter, Material Symbols), fixed top nav, black footer
- `_Sidebar.cshtml` — Fixed left sidebar with ROOT_USER identity block, nav items, UPGRADE_PRO button
- `_ViewImports.cshtml` — Added `@using WebIde.Model` and `@using WebIde.Model.Enums`
- `wwwroot/css/site.css` — Competitive Brutalism utility classes (`.brutal-border`, `.hard-shadow`, `.badge-easy/medium/hard`, `.status-accepted/wrong/tle/error`, `.code-block`, role badges)

### Task 5 — Home page
Created `Models/HomeViewModel.cs` with aggregated stats + top users list.  
Updated `HomeController.cs` to build the view model.  
Ported `homepage/code.html` to `Views/Home/Index.cshtml`:
- Hero: "COMPETE. CODE. CONQUER." with START_CODING / LEADERBOARD CTAs
- Bento grid: Problems 8-col card, Leaderboard 4-col, full-width stats banner with terminal output, stat bars, EXPLORE_ALL card

**User feedback:** "remove the redundant comments. I know what the only 2 divs in the file mean, leave comments on the logic part of the app"  
→ Removed all `<!-- Section Name -->` HTML comments; kept only inline logic comments.

### Task 6 — Problem pages
- `ProblemController.cs` with Index (all problems) and Details (single problem + submissions)
- `Views/Problem/Index.cshtml` — port of `problems_list/code.html`: sidebar, 3 stat cards, filter panel (visual-only), problems table with difficulty badges, tag links, acceptance rate, SOLVE button
- `Views/Problem/Details.cshtml` — port of `solve_workspace/code.html`: split layout, left description pane, right dark editor area showing last submission source, tabbed console with execution result + submissions table

### Task 7 — Leaderboard page
Created `Models/LeaderboardEntry.cs` record type.  
`LeaderboardController.cs` sorts users by Accepted count desc.  
`Views/Leaderboard/Index.cshtml` — port of `leaderboard/code.html`: asymmetric podium top 3 (rank 2 left/lime, rank 1 center elevated/blue, rank 3 right/pink), ranked table for remaining users, stats cards.

**Bug fixed:** Initial controller used anonymous type — not usable as Razor `@model`. Fixed by introducing `LeaderboardEntry` record.

**User feedback on ProblemSetController:** "add this but why do we use the _repo"  
→ Explained `_repo` is the private backing field for the DI-injected singleton; `_` prefix is standard C# convention. Added explanatory comment to the file.

### Task 8 — Remaining entity pages

**User pages:**
- `Views/User/Index.cshtml` — table with avatar initial, display name link, role badge, submission count, accepted count, registered date
- `Views/User/Details.cshtml` — profile card (initial avatar, display name, role badge, email, joined date), 3 stat cards (submissions/accepted/solve rate), orgs list, full submission history table

**Organization pages:**
- `Views/Organization/Index.cshtml` — card grid with hard shadow, member/set counts, VIEW_DETAILS button
- `Views/Organization/Details.cshtml` — full description, stats, members list (with role badges), problem sets list

**ProblemSet pages:**
- `Views/ProblemSet/Index.cshtml` — table with org link, public/private badge, problem count, created date
- `Views/ProblemSet/Details.cshtml` — header with org link, problems table with difficulty badges, tag links, SOLVE button

**Submission pages:**
- `Views/Submission/Index.cshtml` — all submissions table sorted newest-first, user/problem links, language badge, color-coded status chips, score, wall time, peak memory, VIEW button
- `Views/Submission/Details.cshtml` — metadata grid, source code in dark editor block, ExecutionResult section (exit code, timed out, memory exceeded flags, stdout/stderr pre blocks)

**Bugs fixed during build:**
- `MemoryKb` → `PeakMemoryKb` (correct Submission model property)
- `Model.Result` → `Model.ExecutionResult` (correct navigation property name)
- `result.WallTimeMs` → `Model.WallTimeMs` (ExecutionResult has no WallTimeMs)
- `@@user.Username` → `@@@Model.Username` in User/Details (was rendering literal text instead of model value)

**Tag pages:**
- `Views/Tag/Index.cshtml` — responsive 3-column card grid sorted by problem count, hover lift effect, `#TAG_NAME` in primary blue
- `Views/Tag/Details.cshtml` — tag header with count badge, problems table, current tag highlighted in each row's tag list

### Build verification

```
dotnet build WebIde.Web
→ Build succeeded. 0 Warnings. 0 Errors.
```

All 14+ routes verified returning HTTP 200:
`/`, `/Problem`, `/Problem/Details/1–5`, `/Leaderboard`, `/User`, `/User/Details/1–4`,
`/Organization`, `/Organization/Details/1–2`, `/ProblemSet`, `/ProblemSet/Details/1–3`,
`/Submission`, `/Submission/Details/1–8`, `/Tag`, `/Tag/Details/1–6`

Frontend visually verified by user — "the frontend looks good on my end".

---

## Design decisions

| Decision | Rationale |
|---|---|
| Light mode (`#f6f6f6`) not dark IDE | Stitch design kit is explicitly Competitive Brutalism light — takes precedence over initial preference |
| Tailwind CDN (not build step) | No purge needed, all utilities available in Razor views without build tooling |
| `borderRadius: 0px` in Tailwind config | Enforces zero-radius constraint; `full: 9999px` kept for pill shapes only |
| 6 singletons in DI, no scoped/transient | Mock repos are stateless — singleton lifetime appropriate |
| `LeaderboardEntry` record type | Anonymous types can't be used as strongly-typed Razor `@model` |
| `_` prefix on private repo fields | Standard C# convention for private instance fields (`_repo = repo`) |

implement lab 2 - ASP.NET MVC web layer with Competitive Brutalism design

- Add mock repositories (6 singletons) with expanded seed data: 5 problems, 4 users, 2 orgs, 3 problem sets, 8 submissions, 6 tags

- Add UX sub-agent instruction file (.claude/agents/ux-designer.md) and agent invocation log
- Add lab-2/chat-log.md with full implementation conversation record
- Add .gitignore to exclude bin/obj/DS_Store/design assets

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
